Moonshot Daily

66 stocks across 32 sectors — Monday, March 23, 2026

Prices as of 2026-03-23

What’s Hot Today

Monday Morning Moonshot Briefing — March 23, 2026

The big story today: Red across the board. The watchlist bled broadly on Friday's session, with 20 tickers flashing big-move alerts and 11 showing unusual volume spikes. This wasn't sector-specific — it hit biotech, clean energy, uranium, and AI names equally. The most likely culprit: a risk-off rotation as markets digest the latest Fed commentary and rising Treasury yields.

What Stood Out in the Deep Dives

SELLAS Life Sciences (SLS, score: 72) remains the most compelling near-term catalyst play on the entire watchlist. Their REGAL Phase 3 trial has hit 72 of 80 required events — the final readout could literally drop any day. They're sitting on $114M in cash (4+ years of runway), so this isn't a "running out of money" story. The stock pulled back 8.5% on Friday, which could be a gift if the data reads positive. But make no mistake: this is binary. Failure here craters the stock.

PolyPid (PYPD, score: 62) is quietly approaching a huge inflection: their rolling NDA submission is due by end of March — meaning this week or next. A partnership announcement could come alongside it. This one has been under the radar but could move fast.

AST SpaceMobile (ASTS, score: 70) has their BlueBird 7 satellite launch on Blue Origin's New Glenn imminent, with a cadence of one launch every 45 days planned throughout 2026. The space-based cellular network thesis is either visionary or a pipe dream, but the near-term news flow is undeniable.

Danger Zone

Ayr Wellness (AYRWF, score: 12) scored the lowest on the entire watchlist at 12 — the deep dive flagged serious going-concern risk, heavy debt load, and no clear path to profitability. Down 12.5% on Friday. This is the clearest "avoid" signal in the data.

Bloom Energy (BE, score: 57) dropped nearly 10% — the largest dollar-value decline on the board at -$16.57. Still has decent fundamentals but the selloff suggests the market is re-pricing the clean energy space.

Uranium Bright Spot

Ur-Energy (URG, score: 51) was the lone standout gainer, up nearly 10% against the tide. Their Shirley Basin mine startup is imminent (within weeks), which would generate $30M+ in annual free cash flow. This is a real catalyst, not just hype.

The Week Ahead

Multiple earnings reports land March 31: Planet Labs (PLNH), Bitfarms (BITF), and Nano Dimension (NNDM) all report Q4 2025. Each is at an inflection point — PLNH needs to show the California exit is clean, BITF needs to prove the HPC/AI pivot is real, and NNDM's strategic alternatives update could be market-moving.

Bottom line: Despite the broad red day, the catalyst calendar is stacked. SLS's REGAL readout, PYPD's NDA deadline, and three Q4 earnings all land in the next 8 days. This is a week to watch closely, not panic. The deep dives show most positions still have solid fundamentals — this pullback is more macro than micro.

SELLAS Life Sciences
SLS 72/100 Biotech
$4.76
-$0.44 (-8.46%)
Mkt Cap: $855M
Cash: $114.4M ($71.8M year-end 2025 + $42.6M Q1 2026 warrant exercises)
Burn Rate: ~$27M annualized (based on FY2025 net loss; R&D fell to $16M)
Timing: This week / This month — REGAL final analysis could read out any day (72/80 events as of Dec 26, 2025). AACR conference presentation in ~2 weeks (early April) for SLS009 data. This is the highest-immediacy catalyst on the entire batch.
What Changed: Price dropped from ~$5.20 area to $4.76 (-8.46%) on heavy 29.5M volume — pre-readout de-risking. No new REGAL events reported since Dec 26 (still 72/80). Cash position strengthened with $42.6M warrant exercise proceeds. Overall thesis unchanged — binary REGAL readout remains imminent.
Bull case: REGAL Phase 3 final OS analysis is imminent (72/80 events) and extended time-to-event suggests GPS efficacy in AML. Cash position of $114.4M is the strongest in company history, providing ample runway through data readout and potential FDA submission. SLS009 provides pipeline diversification with mid-stage AML/DLBCL data.
What could go wrong: REGAL is a binary event — failure would devastate the stock given $855M market cap is pricing in significant success probability. AML is a competitive space with multiple approved therapies. The company remains pre-revenue with no approved products, and warrant dilution has expanded the share count.
SELLAS Life Sciences: REGAL Study Results Will Be A Major Potential Upside Catalyst

Seeking Alpha analysis highlights that REGAL final OS analysis is imminent with 72/80 events reached, positioning SLS for a potential 50%+ move on readout.

Seeking Alpha

SLS Stock Pops After-Hours As Loss Shrinks, Cash Swells — All Eyes On High Stakes AML Trial In 'Pivotal' 2026

FY2025 net loss narrowed to $26.9M with cash hitting $71.8M; management calls 2026 a 'pivotal year' for the REGAL trial.

Stocktwits

Sellas Life Sciences Draws Significant Retail Buzz As Traders Eye REGAL Phase 3 Catalyst — SLS Stock Soars 15%

Retail investor interest surges ahead of REGAL readout, driving a 15% stock pop on heavy volume.

Stocktwits

SELLAS Life Sciences Reports Full Year 2025 Financial Results and Provides Corporate Update

Cash strengthened to $114.4M after $42.6M in Q1 warrant exercises; R&D costs fell to $16M as company focuses resources on REGAL.

Hims & Hers Health
HIMS 68/100 Digital Health
$22.02
-$2.14 (-8.86%)
Mkt Cap: $5.0B
Cash: $929M total liquidity ($577.5M cash + investments); may tighten after $1.15B Eucalyptus acquisition
Burn Rate: N/A — profitable ($128M net income FY2025, 5.4% margin)
Timing: This month — Q1 2026 earnings likely April 2026 will test whether Novo partnership is driving growth. Post-Novo rally already faded 11% from $24.77 to $22.02.
What Changed: Price dropped to $22.02 (-8.86%) on heavy profit-taking from Novo rally highs. New legal setback reported 3/18. Coordinated insider selling on 3/17 ($2.4M+ by COO). Eucalyptus acquisition ($1.15B) announced — transformative but stresses balance sheet. Barclays raised PT to $29.
Bull case: Novo Nordisk partnership is transformative — direct Ozempic/Wegovy distribution resolves legal dispute and legitimizes HIMS as a healthcare platform. $2.35B revenue (+59%), $128M net income, $929M liquidity. Branded GLP-1 distribution creates regulatory moat vs. Ro/Amazon Pharmacy. Eucalyptus acquisition ($1.15B) adds 500K+ international subscribers. Barclays PT $29 (+32% upside). GLP-1 market TAM is $100B+.
What could go wrong: Q1 2026 guidance ($600-625M) missed consensus ($653M) — growth decelerating from 59% to ~15%. Free cash flow collapsed 71% ($198M→$57M) despite profitability. 82 insider sells vs 0 buys is extreme negative signal; COO dumped $2.4M on 3/17. 'Another legal setback' (3/18) suggests unresolved compliance probes. CEO/leadership changes during $1.15B Eucalyptus integration creates execution risk. Balance sheet may be stressed post-acquisition.
Hims & Hers Officially Launches Massive New Product

New product launch post-Novo partnership positions HIMS as first-mover in branded GLP-1 telehealth distribution.

Motley Fool

Hims Faces Another Legal Setback Amid Probes

Ongoing legal/compliance challenges beyond the Novo settlement create regulatory uncertainty.

StockToTrade

Novo Nordisk Partnership — Stock Skyrockets 40%

Peace treaty deal gives HIMS direct Ozempic/Wegovy access, resolving legal overhang and opening massive GLP-1 distribution channel.

StockToTrade

Barclays Raises Hims & Hers Target to $29 After Novo Deal Clears Legal Overhang

Analyst Glen Santangelo sees Novo deal removing risk; maintains Overweight with 32% upside target.

24/7 Wall St.

Hims & Hers Reports Q4 and Full Year 2025 Financial Results

Revenue $2.35B (+59%), net income $128M, but Q1 2026 guidance $600-625M missed $653M consensus. FCF collapsed 71%.

ASTS 68/100 Space / Telecom
$89.93
-$4.16 (-4.42%)
Mkt Cap: $34.4B
Cash: $3.9B pro forma (year-end 2025; raised $3.5B+ in 2025)
Burn Rate: $67M+/quarter (Q4 net loss $74M, Q3 net loss $123M)
Timing: This month — BlueBird 7 launch on Blue Origin New Glenn imminent. Launch cadence targeting every 45 days throughout 2026. Each successful launch is a catalyst. Next earnings May 18, 2026.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: $3.9B cash funds aggressive constellation build-out of 45-60 satellites in 2026. $1.2B contracted revenue pipeline provides visibility. BlueBird satellites achieving 120 Mbps speeds with 2,400 sq ft phased arrays — technology is working. TELUS partnership and SDA contract validate both commercial and government demand. Deutsche Bank target $139 implies 55% upside.
What could go wrong: $34.4B market cap on $70.9M 2025 revenue (486x sales) is extreme. Massive insider selling (-$161.4M in 90 days) is a red flag. Execution risk on launching 45-60 satellites in one year is enormous. Q4 EPS missed estimates by 36%. Revenue still minimal relative to constellation cost.
Deutsche Bank Elevates AST SpaceMobile Price Target to $139

Analyst sees growth confidence in constellation build-out and commercial partnerships. 55% upside from current price.

Deutsche Bank / MarketBeat

TELUS Partnership for Canada Space-Based Broadband

Canadian telecom giant becomes equity shareholder and commits to space-based broadband service planned for late 2026.

AST SpaceMobile Wins ~$30M SDA Contract for Europa Track 2 HALO Program

U.S. Space Development Agency contract for on-orbit demonstrations using BlueBird LEO constellation. Government revenue diversification.

BlueBird 6 Launch Success — Largest Commercial LEO Array Deployed

2,400 sq ft phased array achieving peak speeds of 120 Mbps. Milestone validates space-based cellular technology.

BlueBird 7 Encapsulated for Blue Origin New Glenn Rocket

Second satellite ready for inaugural New Glenn mission. Launch cadence targeting every 45 days in 2026.

Rapid7
RPD 66/100 Cybersecurity
$6.31
+$0.00 (+0.00%)
Mkt Cap: $416M
Cash: Strong — $130.1M free cash flow FY2025; $151.8M non-GAAP net income
Burn Rate: N/A — cash flow positive ($130.1M FCF FY2025)
Timing: This quarter — Q1 2026 earnings will test whether guidance cut was conservative or realistic. Jana Partners activist involvement could drive strategic announcements. 180% analyst upside suggests potential re-rating catalyst, but timing unclear.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Massive valuation disconnect — $860M revenue, $130M FCF, $151M non-GAAP net income at ~$6.31 stock price implies extreme undervaluation. CEO buying 14,500 shares signals management confidence. Jana Partners activist accumulation (813K shares) suggests potential for strategic action (go-private, restructuring, or operational improvement). Cybersecurity demand is secular — 105% surge in exploited vulnerabilities validates TAM. New cloud security products (Exposure Command, DSPM) address growing market. Analyst avg PT $17.71 (+180% upside from $6.31).
What could go wrong: 2026 guidance of $835-843M implies YoY revenue DECLINE (-2-3%) — first decline signals structural demand issues, not just macro. ARR flat at $840M means no net new business growth. Stock crashed 33.6% on guidance cut — market pricing in execution failure. Hold consensus (16 of 21 analysts) shows Wall Street skepticism. Company-specific challenges appear separate from cybersecurity sector strength. Could be value trap if revenue decline accelerates.
Rapid7 Outlines $835M-$843M 2026 Revenue Guidance as AI-Driven Security Investments Continue

Guidance below consensus ($869.7M) triggered 33.6% stock crash. Revenue declining YoY despite cybersecurity demand growth. Non-GAAP EPS $1.50-1.60.

Seeking Alpha

Rapid7 2026 Global Threat Landscape Report Shows Exploited Vulnerabilities Surged 105%

Attack surface expanding rapidly — 146 high/critical vulns exploited in 2025 vs 71 in 2024. Validates cybersecurity spending need.

GlobeNewsWire

Rapid7 Launches New Exposure Command Cloud Security Capabilities

Runtime validation and DSPM additions expand cloud security platform — critical for maintaining competitive positioning.

Rapid7

Rapid7 Overhauls PACT Partner Program with New Platinum Tier

Simplified deal registration and enhanced partner economics target partner-led MDR growth to offset direct sales challenges.

Cingulate Inc.
CING 65/100 Biotech / Pharma
$7.38
-$0.13 (-1.73%)
Mkt Cap: $81M
Cash: ~$23M ($11M year-end 2025 + $12M PIPE closed February 2026)
Burn Rate: ~$22.4M annualized (FY2025 net loss)
Timing: This quarter — PDUFA date of May 31, 2026 is the definitive binary catalyst, ~10 weeks away. Stock could move 100%+ on approval or -50%+ on CRL. This is the highest-conviction timing call in the batch after SLS.
What Changed: Price stabilized at $7.38 from recent high of $11.66 (-37% correction). Cash situation improved with $12M PIPE closing but remains tight at ~12 months runway. Patent Notice of Allowance is new positive (March 17, protection through 2042). No change to PDUFA date or clinical data. The risk/reward profile remains highly binary.
Bull case: CTx-1301 has a firm FDA PDUFA date of May 31, 2026 — binary approval catalyst in ~10 weeks. Phase 3 data showed statistically significant efficacy with entire active-day symptom control, differentiating from existing ADHD meds. ADHD market is massive ($25B+ globally). Patent protection through 2042. $12M PIPE with insider participation and lock-up shows alignment. $4.3M PDUFA fee waiver is unusual FDA accommodation. Analyst target of $37 implies ~400% upside.
What could go wrong: CRITICAL: Cash of ~$23M with $22.4M annual burn gives only ~12 months runway. Will need significant financing for commercial launch even if approved. $81M market cap is tiny — high volatility and liquidity risk. Manufacturing agreement with Bend Bio Sciences is in place but commercial scale-up unproven. FDA could issue a Complete Response Letter. Stock fell from $11.66 to $7.38 (-37%) in days, suggesting some holders taking profits.
FDA Accepts Cingulate's New Drug Application for CTx-1301 in ADHD and Sets May 31, 2026 PDUFA Date

NDA accepted with PDUFA fee waiver; binary FDA decision in ~10 weeks will determine company's future.

GlobeNewsWire

Cingulate Closes $12 Million Private Placement

$12M PIPE at market price with insider participation and 180-day lock-up; provides bridge financing through PDUFA date.

StockTitan

Cingulate Presents Positive Phase 3 Results for CTx-1301 Demonstrating Statistically Significant Efficacy and Entire Active-Day Symptom Control

Phase 3 shows large effect size measurements throughout the day with rapid onset — key differentiator for once-daily ADHD treatment.

Cingulate IR

United States Patent and Trademark Office Issues Notice of Allowance for CTx-1301 Patent

Patent protection through May 2042 secures IP moat for CTx-1301 ahead of potential FDA approval.

Nova Ltd.
NVMI 65/100 Semiconductors
$465.30
-$6.78 (-1.44%)
Mkt Cap: $14.8B
Cash: Profitable — record revenue with strong margins; specific cash balance not disclosed in search results
Burn Rate: N/A — profitable semiconductor metrology company
Timing: This quarter — Earnings May 7, 2026 will validate $1B revenue trajectory. Metrion adoption announcements could accelerate design win momentum. CFO investor roadshow (Feb-Mar) suggests confidence in near-term outlook.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Nova is the metrology leader for advanced semiconductor nodes with Metrion platform wins at two leading global manufacturers for GAA and DRAM. Revenue $880.58M (+31%) targeting $1B milestone. Metrology intensity increases with every advanced node transition — structural demand tailwind. Barclays initiated Overweight (March 11). Institutional accumulation (Vanguard, Quantbot). Israeli origin provides favorable export control positioning. Q1 guidance $222-232M implies $1B+ run rate.
What could go wrong: Stock at $465.30 already trades above analyst average target of $427 — limited near-term upside per consensus. If WFE spending surprises to downside, stock could retreat to $380-400. Low single-digit WFE growth outlook for 2026 may cap upside. Competition from Applied Materials' metrology push. At 7 Buy/2 Hold, consensus is bullish but stock may already reflect this. Semi equipment cyclicality risk if AI capex peaks.
Nova Announces Adoption of Metrion by Two Leading Global Manufacturers

Metrion platform selected for Gate-All-Around and advanced DRAM production — validates next-gen metrology leadership.

PR Newswire

Nova Stock Up 8.6% on Barclays Overweight Initiation

Analyst Tom O'Malley initiates coverage highlighting metrology-intensive manufacturing tailwind and favorable demand cycle.

Oscar Health
OSCR 63/100 Digital Health
$12.63
-$0.67 (-5.04%)
Mkt Cap: $3.8B
Cash: Strong — $475M secured revolving credit facility (Feb 2026); 55% of required capital covered by reinsurance partners
Burn Rate: N/A — approaching profitability ($250-450M operating income guided for 2026); FY2025 net loss $443.2M
Timing: This quarter — Q1 2026 earnings on May 6 is the critical profitability test. If EPS is positive and MLR shows improvement, stock reprices higher. Guidance reaffirmation at March conference is encouraging but unproven.
What Changed: Price dropped to $12.63 (-5.04%) continuing downtrend from $15.05 peak. Management reaffirmed 2026 outlook at March investor conference. New $475M credit facility secured Feb 6. CTO Schlosser sold $3.6M in January. No material change in fundamentals since last deep dive — execution remains the key variable.
Bull case: Revenue guiding to $18.7-19.0B (+60% YoY) with $250-450M operating income — massive scale inflection from -$443M loss. 3.4M members (+58% YoY) with CVS/Aetna exiting individual ACA market driving share gains. Oswell AI completing 86% of member questions autonomously and reducing response times 67%. MLR compression from 95.4% to 82.4-83.4% target would transform unit economics. Analyst avg PT $15.40-17.50 (22-39% upside).
What could go wrong: Q4 2025 EPS miss (-$1.24 vs -$0.89) raised execution concerns. $443M net loss on $11.7B revenue shows razor-thin or negative margins at scale. Required 12-point MLR compression from 95.4% to 82.4-83.4% is historically aggressive. CTO sold $3.6M in stock with no insider buying. ACA market may shrink under political pressure. Rapid member growth from CVS exit may include sicker, price-sensitive members (adverse selection). 28% rate increases could face state regulatory pushback.
Oscar Health's CEO Says 2026 Is the Year It Finally Turns a Profit

CEO betting on MLR compression, AI efficiency, and 28% rate increases to deliver $250-450M operating income — first profitable year ever.

24/7 Wall St.

Did Oscar's Reaffirmed 2026 Outlook and Reinsurance Strategy Just Shift Oscar Health's Investment Narrative?

55% of capital covered by reinsurance partners de-risks balance sheet. Management stands behind $18.7-19.0B revenue guidance.

Oscar Health Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Results

Revenue $11.7B, 3.4M members (+58%), but net loss of $443.2M. Q4 EPS -$1.24 missed estimates.

New credit facility optimizes capital structure ahead of 2026 profitability push.

Innodata Inc.
INOD 62/100 AI / Data
$43.66
-$0.77 (-1.73%)
Mkt Cap: $1.4B
Cash: $82.2M cash and short-term investments (up from $46.9M YoY)
Burn Rate: Profitable — $32.2M net income FY2025; $57.9M adj. EBITDA
Timing: This quarter — Watch Q1 2026 earnings to see if $68M in new wins translates to revenue acceleration. Federal AI spending legislation could be a catalyst.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Profitable and growing at 48% YoY with 2026 guidance of 35%+ growth. $68M in new pre-training data contract wins signal accelerating demand from Big Tech AI customers. $25M federal contract opens government AI market. $82.2M cash (nearly doubled YoY) with positive cash flow means no dilution risk. At $1.4B market cap and ~$340M estimated 2026 revenue, trades at ~4x forward revenue — cheap for an AI services company growing 35%+.
What could go wrong: Revenue concentration risk — dependent on a few Big Tech customers for AI training data. Q4 growth decelerated to 22% from 48% full-year pace, raising questions about sustainability. AI training data is somewhat commoditizable long-term as synthetic data improves. Federal contracts can be lumpy and politically dependent. Small company ($1.4B cap) competing for contracts with much larger rivals.
Innodata 2025 revenue jumps 48%, sees 35% in 2026

Full-year revenue hit $251.7M with $32.2M net income. 2026 guidance of 35%+ growth with potential upside as programs scale.

StockTitan

Do Expanding Big-Tech Contracts Push Innodata Toward a Stronger 2026?

Analysis of how $68M in new pre-training data wins and federal contracts position INOD for accelerating growth in 2026.

Nasdaq/Zacks

Innodata reiterates 45%+ 2025 growth guidance as new federal and AI contracts signal transformative 2026 outlook

New $25M federal customer project and Big Tech expansion signal multiple growth vectors for 2026.

Seeking Alpha

Is Innodata Well-Positioned for America's Accelerating AI Spend?

Analysis argues INOD's data services are well-positioned to capture increasing AI infrastructure spending across government and enterprise sectors.

Nasdaq

Insider Activity: unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown
Allogene Therapeutics
ALLO 62/100 Biotech
$2.07
-$0.15 (-6.76%)
Mkt Cap: $465M
Cash: $258.3M (cash, equivalents, and investments at year-end 2025)
Burn Rate: ~$150M annualized operating cash expense (2026 guidance); GAAP OpEx ~$210M including $35M non-cash SBC
Timing: This month — ALPHA3 futility analysis expected April 2026 (~2-4 weeks). This is the single most important catalyst for the stock. ALLO-329 POC data by June 2026 provides second catalyst.
What Changed: Price dropped from $2.22 to $2.07 (-6.76% today) on heavy 20.7M volume. No new data since prior deep dive. ALPHA3 futility timeline unchanged at April 2026. Cash runway still into Q1 2028. Insider sales were routine tax-related, not discretionary. Downward price trend continues.
Bull case: ALPHA3 futility analysis in April 2026 is the nearest binary catalyst — a pass validates first-line allogeneic CAR T in LBCL, a massive market. Arbitration win clears path to full global cema-cel rights. ALLO-329 dual CD19/CD70 program entering autoimmune space (SLE/lupus) with H1 2026 POC data. $258M cash provides runway into 2028.
What could go wrong: ALPHA3 futility analysis is binary — failure would devastate the stock (already at $2.07). Allogeneic CAR T has historically underperformed autologous approaches in durability. $150M annual burn with pre-revenue status means ongoing dilution risk. Stock down from $2.78 to $2.07 in 20 days (-25%), suggesting market skepticism.
Allogene Therapeutics Positions 2026 as a Program-Defining Year for Scalable, Real-World Allogeneic CAR T

Company outlines H1 2026 catalyst stack: ALPHA3 futility analysis in early Q2 and ALLO-329 autoimmune POC by mid-year.

GlobeNewsWire

Piper Sandler Analysts Bullish on Allogene Therapeutics Ahead of Key ALPHA3 Data Readout

Analysts highlight ALPHA3 futility analysis as the most important near-term catalyst, maintaining bullish stance.

Insider Monkey

Allogene Therapeutics Reports Favorable Result for Servier in Arbitration with Cellectis

Arbitration win reconfirms Allogene's full development and commercial control of cema-cel in US, EU, and UK.

GlobeNewsWire

Insider Selling: Allogene Therapeutics CEO Sells $117,974.61 in Stock

CEO sold shares to cover tax withholding on RSU vesting — mandatory transaction, not discretionary selling.

Daily Political

Ballard Power Systems
BLDP 62/100 Clean Energy
$2.41
-$0.07 (-2.82%)
Mkt Cap: $725M
Cash: $525.7M cash, no bank debt, current ratio 8.33
Burn Rate: Operating costs reduced 41% in Q4 2025; targeting 30% further reduction in 2026
Timing: This quarter to 6 months — Q2-Q4 2026 catalyst window: New Flyer deliveries begin, H2 revenue ramp materializes. Breakeven timing depends on mix and pricing power vs BEV alternatives.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Ballard achieved 43% revenue growth to $99.4M with 5% positive gross margins and record Q4 OCF. New Flyer's 500-engine order (50 MW) validates commercial hydrogen bus demand. Guidance targets H2 2026 revenue surge with 30% opex cuts, positioning for profitability inflection.
What could go wrong: Hydrogen infrastructure adoption remains uncertain; fuel cell bus penetration competes with battery electric alternatives. Contract pipeline visibility beyond 2026 unclear. Capital intensity of manufacturing ramp could pressure margins. Geopolitical risks to supply chains.
Ballard Reports Q4 2025 Revenue +37% QoQ, Full Year $99.4M Up 43% YoY

Q4 gross margin 17%, highest in decade; full year positive OCF signals path to sustainability.

PR Newswire

New Flyer Orders 500 FCmove-HD+ Engines (50 MW) - Largest Commitment to Date

Xcelsior CHARGE FC hydrogen buses to begin deliveries in 2026; validates commercial hydrogen adoption.

InvestorIdeas

eCap Marine Selects 6.4 MW Ballard Fuel Cell Engines for Samskip Vessels

32 FCwave-200kW engines for maritime applications; expands addressable market beyond transit.

Ballard Press Release

Insider Activity: unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown
EHang Holdings
EH 61/100 Aerospace / eVTOL
$9.95
-$0.92 (-8.46%)
Mkt Cap: $716M
Cash: RMB 1.13B (~$157M) as of Dec 31, 2025
Burn Rate: Approaching breakeven — Q4 2025 was first GAAP profitable quarter. TTM shows -$230.54M loss but improving trajectory
Timing: This quarter — Thailand commercial license expected Q2 2026. Commercial ticketing already live in China (March 2026). VT35 certification milestones throughout 2026. Next earnings ~May 26, 2026.
What Changed: Commercial ticketing LAUNCHED in Guangzhou and Hefei (Mar 2026 — NEW). Thailand commercial license imminent (Q2 2026 target — NEW). New CTO Shuai Feng appointed (Jan 14 — NEW). Stock dropped 19% from $12.25 to $9.95 despite positive operational milestones. 2026 revenue guidance of RMB 600M (+18%) confirmed.
Bull case: Only eVTOL company globally with type certification + commercial deliveries + GAAP profitability in same quarter. 221 units delivered in 2025 (100 in Q4 alone) demonstrates manufacturing scale. International expansion accelerating — Thailand, Qatar, Saudi Arabia. VT35 long-range model (200km range) opens new market segments. Analyst consensus 'Strong Buy' with average target $21-25 (110-150% upside from $9.95).
What could go wrong: Stock down 19% in 30 days from $12.25 to $9.95 — significant momentum deterioration. 2026 revenue guidance of RMB 600M (+18%) is modest growth for a growth stock. $716M market cap in Chinese regulatory environment carries geopolitical risk. TTM still shows large loss despite Q4 profitability. Wide analyst EPS range (-$1.36 to $3.76) signals uncertainty.
EHang Commences Public Ticketing for Commercial Aerial Sightseeing in Guangzhou and Hefei

First public ticketing for autonomous eVTOL flights. Two operators with Air Operator Certificates now active in China.

EHang Newsroom

EHang Reports Record Q4: 100 Unit Deliveries, First GAAP Profitable Quarter

221 total 2025 deliveries. Revenue +48.4% QoQ. First quarter of GAAP profitability in company history.

EHang IR

Thailand Expected to Issue First Pilotless eVTOL Commercial License

Civil Aviation Authority of Thailand targeting Q2 2026 for commercial operation license. First international market for autonomous eVTOL.

EHang Newsroom

EHang Signs Thailand UAM MOU with Bangkok Land and China Harbour Engineering

Ecosystem development for urban air mobility in Thailand. Met with Thai Deputy PM and Ministry of Transport.

EHang Newsroom

EHang Appoints Shuai Feng as Chief Technology Officer

Founding team member since 2014 who led development of EH216-S and VT35. Signals focus on next-gen product development.

EHang IR

Insider Activity: unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown
Joby Aviation
JOBY 61/100 Aerospace / eVTOL
$9.23
-$0.46 (-4.75%)
Mkt Cap: $9.0B
Cash: $1.4B cash/short-term investments (Q4 2025; recently raised $1.2B in common stock + convertible notes)
Burn Rate: $700-740M annualized (H1 2026 guidance $340-370M + $33M building purchase)
Timing: This quarter to H2 2026 — TIA testing active and progressing rapidly (18-point jump in Q4). Dubai launch expected 2026. White House pilot program operations starting. Each FAA milestone is a catalyst. Next earnings likely May 2026.
What Changed: Cash position DOWN to $1.4B (from $2.6B baseline). $1.2B raised in common stock + convertible notes. Stage 4 progress at 80% confirmed (18-point Q4 jump is new). San Francisco Bay demo flights completed (Mar 2026). Runway shortened to ~23 months. Q4 revenue beat consensus ($30.84M vs $16.88M expected).
Bull case: US eVTOL certification frontrunner: Stage 4 at 80%+ complete with 18-point Q4 jump. FAA-conforming aircraft flying since March 11. White House pilot program across 10 states validates technology at highest level. Uber Air partnership provides demand channel. $1.4B cash + $1.2B recent raise ensures runway through certification. Dubai launch in 2026 opens international revenue.
What could go wrong: Cash position dropped from $2.6B to $1.4B — $700-740M annual burn is enormous for a company with $31M quarterly Blade revenue. 23-month runway at current burn means certification must happen soon or more dilution needed. FAA certification has slipped before. $9B market cap on minimal own-aircraft revenue requires faith in certification timeline.
Joby Aviation's First FAA-Conforming Aircraft Enters Stage 4 TIA Testing

Aircraft N547JX flew March 11. Stage 4 at 80% complete (Joby side), 73% (FAA side). 18-point progress jump in Q4.

Joby Aviation IR

Joby Selected for White House eVTOL Integration Pilot Program Across 10 States

Arizona, Florida, Idaho, NJ, NY, NC, Oklahoma, Oregon, Texas, Utah. Highest-level government validation of eVTOL technology.

Joby Aviation Newsroom

Joby Completes Piloted Demo Flights Around San Francisco Bay

Public demonstration of air taxi capability in major US metro. Builds public acceptance and regulatory confidence.

Joby Aviation Newsroom

Joby Q4 2025: Revenue Beats Consensus at $30.84M vs $16.88M Expected

Blade helicopter operations provide bridge revenue. Cash position at $1.4B with $1.2B recently raised.

Public.com

Uber Air Powered by Joby Now Bookable in Uber App

Ride-hailing integration creates immediate demand channel for commercial operations once certified.

Joby Aviation Newsroom

Sezzle Inc.
SEZL 59/100 Fintech
$66.15
-$0.09 (-0.14%)
Mkt Cap: $2.3B
Cash: $104-135M (end of FY2025, with $33.1M operational cash flow and $134.7M end cash position)
Burn Rate: HIGHLY PROFITABLE — $133.1M net income FY2025. No burn rate; strong cash generation.
Timing: This quarter — Sezzle Mobile launch is the next major catalyst. Q1 2026 earnings will show if 25-30% growth guidance is on track. Regulatory developments around BNPL could impact sentiment.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Sezzle is a rare BNPL company that's actually highly profitable — $133M net income on $450M revenue (30% net margin). 25-30% revenue growth guided for 2026 with Sezzle Mobile launch (AT&T partnership) as a new growth vector. Subscriber growth +30% YoY. Non-GAAP gross margin of 64.3% in Q4. Expanding from BNPL into an 'all-in-one' consumer financial app (deposits, credit cards, mobile plans). Credit loss provision at only ~2% of GMV shows disciplined underwriting.
What could go wrong: Every C-suite executive is selling stock — CEO, COO, CFO, President all sold in Feb-March 2026. Material weakness in financial controls is a serious governance flag. Stock spiked from $56.50 to $84.70 (+50%) then fell back to $66.15 — significant volatility. BNPL regulatory scrutiny is intensifying. Sezzle Mobile is unproven — phone service is a completely different business. At $2.3B market cap / $133M earnings = 17x P/E, not cheap for a BNPL company facing potential regulatory headwinds.
Sezzle Q4 Profits Surge on Strong Margins, Company Raises 2026 Guidance

Beat estimates with $133M net income on $450M revenue. Guided 25-30% growth for 2026. Stock surged 26% on results.

SignalBloom AI

Sezzle (SEZL) Is Up 16.3% After 2025 Earnings Beat And Sezzle Mobile Launch Guidance

Earnings beat combined with Sezzle Mobile announcement (AT&T partnership, $29.99/mo plans) drove significant stock appreciation.

Simply Wall St

TD Cowen Revises Sezzle's Price Target Amid CFO Appointment

New CFO appointment and coverage update. Analyst attention growing as Sezzle scales beyond pure BNPL.

StocksToTrade

Sezzle's Top Brass Make Bold Moves With Major Insider Stock Sales

CEO, COO, CFO, and President all sold stock in Feb-March 2026. All under pre-planned 10b5-1 trading plans.

TipRanks

Upstart Holdings
UPST 58/100 AI / Fintech
$25.98
-$0.79 (-2.95%)
Mkt Cap: $2.5B
Cash: Data unavailable for exact cash figure; funded through ABS market and lending partners
Burn Rate: Profitable — FY2025 net income $54M, adj. EBITDA $230M (22% margin)
Timing: This quarter — Q1 2026 earnings May 12 will show momentum. Bank charter application process is 6+ months. Small business lending launch in late 2026 is a back-half catalyst.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Revenue growing 64% YoY with profitability ($54M net income) — rare combination in fintech. Bank charter application is transformative: enables deposit funding, direct lending, lower costs, and regulatory simplification. $1.4B 2026 revenue guidance with 25% EBITDA margin target by 2028. AI-driven underwriting is proven with $11B in origination volume (+86% YoY). Cash Line and small business lending open new massive TAMs.
What could go wrong: Bank charter approval is uncertain and could take 12-18 months — regulatory risk is real. Stock down ~5% over 20 sessions. Credit cycle sensitivity — AI lending models untested through a deep recession. Heavy reliance on ABS market for funding creates liquidity risk if credit markets tighten. $2.5B market cap on ~$1.4B 2026 revenue = ~1.8x forward sales, but regulatory overhang caps multiple.
Upstart to Apply for National Bank Charter

Upstart announced application to OCC and FDIC to establish Upstart Bank, N.A., enabling deposit funding and direct lending. CTO Paul Gu calls it 'the first bank built from the ground up on AI.'

BusinessWire

Upstart (UPST) at the Crossroads: AI Lending, Bank Charters, and the New Era of Credit

In-depth research feature analyzing how bank charter could transform Upstart's economics and competitive position in AI lending.

FinancialContent

Will Cash Line Launch and Early 2026 Origination Momentum Change Upstart Holdings' Narrative?

Cash Line ($200-$5K revolving credit) launched February 2026, targeting payday lender disruption. Early origination data is encouraging.

Simply Wall St

Upstart Holdings Q4 2025 Earnings Beat

Q4 EPS of $0.46 beat estimates by 35%. Revenue of $296M (+35% YoY). Full-year origination volume reached $11B (+86% YoY).

Nasdaq

Upstart to Apply for National Bank Charter

Upstart announced application to OCC and FDIC to establish Upstart Bank, N.A., enabling deposit funding and direct lending. CTO Paul Gu calls it 'the first bank built from the ground up on AI.'

BusinessWire

Upstart (UPST) at the Crossroads: AI Lending, Bank Charters, and the New Era of Credit

In-depth research feature analyzing how bank charter could transform Upstart's economics and competitive position in AI lending.

FinancialContent

Will Cash Line Launch and Early 2026 Origination Momentum Change Upstart Holdings' Narrative?

Cash Line ($200-$5K revolving credit) launched February 2026, targeting payday lender disruption. Early origination data is encouraging.

Simply Wall St

Upstart Holdings Q4 2025 Earnings Beat

Q4 EPS of $0.46 beat estimates by 35%. Revenue of $296M (+35% YoY). Full-year origination volume reached $11B (+86% YoY).

Taysha Gene Therapies
TSHA 58/100 Biotech
$4.40
-$0.16 (-3.51%)
Mkt Cap: $1.2B
Cash: $319.8M (cash, equivalents as of year-end 2025, with $297.3M reported at Q3 2025 and additional funding)
Burn Rate: ~$110M annualized (estimated from negative net margin and operating expenses)
Timing: This quarter — REVEAL pivotal dosing completion and longer-term Phase 1/2 safety/efficacy update both expected Q2 2026. BLA submission likely H2 2026 or H1 2027. Meaningful stock catalyst in next 2-3 months.
What Changed: Price stable at $4.40 (-3.5% today). No material changes from prior deep dive. FY2025 results reported March 19 with Q4 beat. REVEAL enrollment continues on track. Cash runway remains into 2028. Thesis intact.
Bull case: TSHA-102 for Rett syndrome has FDA alignment on BLA pathway with potential broad label (ages 2+). REVEAL pivotal dosing completes Q2 2026 with clean safety profile (no SAEs/DLTs). $319.8M cash provides ~3-year runway through potential approval. Analyst consensus target of ~$12 implies 173% upside from $4.40.
What could go wrong: Gene therapy manufacturing and delivery risks remain. Rett syndrome is a small patient population (~15,000 in US) limiting commercial upside. Competition from Neurogene's gene therapy program. Negative net margin of -1,145% reflects massive burn relative to revenue. Single-program dependency.
Taysha Gene Therapies Reports Full-Year 2025 Financial Results and Provides Corporate Update

Q4 2025 EPS of -$0.087 beat consensus of -$0.096; revenue of $5.49M well above estimates. Cash position supports operations into 2028.

GlobeNewsWire

Taysha Gene Therapies Announces Progress Across TSHA-102 Pivotal Gene Therapy Program in Rett Syndrome

First patient dosed in REVEAL pivotal trial Q4 2025; dosing completion expected Q2 2026 across REVEAL and ASPIRE trials.

GlobeNewsWire

Taysha Gene Therapies (NASDAQ:TSHA) Stock Rating Upgraded by Wall Street Zen

Rating upgrade reflects positive REVEAL trial momentum and strong cash position supporting multiple clinical milestones.

Defense World

Phase 1/2 Studies Highlight Durable Impacts of Gene Therapy TSHA-102 in Rett Syndrome

Low and high dose TSHA-102 show durable improvements with no treatment-related SAEs; supports advancement to pivotal trial.

NeurologyLive

Cash: Data unavailable for exact cash figure; funded through ABS market and lending partners
Burn Rate: Profitable — FY2025 net income $54M, adj. EBITDA $230M (22% margin)
Timing: This quarter — Q1 2026 earnings May 12 will show momentum. Bank charter application process is 6+ months. Small business lending launch in late 2026 is a back-half catalyst.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Revenue growing 64% YoY with profitability ($54M net income) — rare combination in fintech. Bank charter application is transformative: enables deposit funding, direct lending, lower costs, and regulatory simplification. $1.4B 2026 revenue guidance with 25% EBITDA margin target by 2028. AI-driven underwriting is proven with $11B in origination volume (+86% YoY). Cash Line and small business lending open new massive TAMs.
What could go wrong: Bank charter approval is uncertain and could take 12-18 months — regulatory risk is real. Stock down ~5% over 20 sessions. Credit cycle sensitivity — AI lending models untested through a deep recession. Heavy reliance on ABS market for funding creates liquidity risk if credit markets tighten. $2.5B market cap on ~$1.4B 2026 revenue = ~1.8x forward sales, but regulatory overhang caps multiple.
Upstart to Apply for National Bank Charter

Upstart announced application to OCC and FDIC to establish Upstart Bank, N.A., enabling deposit funding and direct lending. CTO Paul Gu calls it 'the first bank built from the ground up on AI.'

BusinessWire

Upstart (UPST) at the Crossroads: AI Lending, Bank Charters, and the New Era of Credit

In-depth research feature analyzing how bank charter could transform Upstart's economics and competitive position in AI lending.

FinancialContent

Will Cash Line Launch and Early 2026 Origination Momentum Change Upstart Holdings' Narrative?

Cash Line ($200-$5K revolving credit) launched February 2026, targeting payday lender disruption. Early origination data is encouraging.

Simply Wall St

Upstart Holdings Q4 2025 Earnings Beat

Q4 EPS of $0.46 beat estimates by 35%. Revenue of $296M (+35% YoY). Full-year origination volume reached $11B (+86% YoY).

Nasdaq

Upstart to Apply for National Bank Charter

Upstart announced application to OCC and FDIC to establish Upstart Bank, N.A., enabling deposit funding and direct lending. CTO Paul Gu calls it 'the first bank built from the ground up on AI.'

NexGen Energy
NXE 58/100 Mining / Uranium
$11.26
-$0.13 (-1.14%)
Mkt Cap: $7.4B
Cash: Data unavailable (exact figure not disclosed). Described as 'flawless balance sheet' with sufficient funds for Rook I construction start.
Burn Rate: C$309M net loss FY2025 (wider than C$77.6M FY2024, includes non-cash items)
Timing: This quarter — summer 2026 Rook I construction start is the imminent transformational catalyst. Progress updates on procurement and permitting drive near-term sentiment.
What Changed: Price dropped from $11.39 to $11.26 (-1.14% today). Volume spike at 34.7M shares. No new fundamental catalysts since last deep dive but $7.3M insider purchase (March 22) is a powerful new signal. Rook I timeline remains on track for summer 2026.
Bull case: Rook I is the world's highest-grade, largest undeveloped uranium deposit with 30M lbs/yr production capacity. Construction start targeted summer 2026 is a transformational catalyst. $7.3M insider purchase is the strongest bullish insider signal on the entire watchlist. CAD$2.2B CapEx estimate maintained. 28 RFP packages issued with critical-path procurement advanced. AI data center demand is structural tailwind for uranium.
What could go wrong: Pre-revenue company with C$309M net loss and EPS miss in Q4. CAD$2.2B construction mega-project carries enormous cost overrun risk — common in mining. Regulatory risk in Saskatchewan remains. Uranium price dependency — economics weaken below $50/lb spot. Long timeline to first production (2028-2029 at earliest). Stock down 9% in 20 days from $12.39 to $11.26.
NexGen Energy Up 123% This Past Year as Investor Adds $7.3 Million Before Major Approval

Significant $7.3M insider purchase ahead of Rook I construction approval. Stock momentum reflects growing confidence in project timeline.

Motley Fool

Assessing NexGen Energy's Valuation As 2026 Becomes Pivotal For Rook I Progress

2026 is make-or-break year with construction start targeted for summer. Valuation premium reflects world-class deposit quality.

Simply Wall St

NexGen Energy Releases Quarterly Earnings Results

Q4 EPS of -$0.05 missed consensus of -$0.03. Net loss widened to C$309M. But Rook I progress on track.

Stock Observer

NexGen Energy's Shares Up After Jim Cramer Said You Can Let It Run

Cramer endorsement drives retail interest. 90-day return of 43.4% and 1-year return of 161.5%.

Yahoo Finance

IonQ
IONQ 58/100 Quantum Computing
$31.20
-$0.70 (-2.19%)
Mkt Cap: $11.5B
Cash: $3.3B (cash & investments, Dec 31 2025)
Burn Rate: $310-330M annualized (2026 adj. EBITDA guidance)
Timing: This quarter — SkyWater acquisition expected to close Q2-Q3 2026. SHIELD task order awards could come any time. Valuation compression risk if growth slows.
What Changed: Prior deep dive existed. Cash position confirmed at $3.3B (stronger than 'funded through contracts' description). RPO detail added ($370M, 4.8x growth). SkyWater acquisition ($1.8B) is major new development. Insider activity confirmed routine. Price declined from $40.88 peak to $31.20 (-24%).
Bull case: Revenue grew 202% to $130M in 2025 with guidance for $225-245M in 2026. $3.3B cash provides 10+ years runway. RPO surged 4.8x to $370M showing multi-quarter demand visibility. MDA SHIELD contract and SkyWater acquisition ($1.8B) create vertically integrated quantum-defense platform.
What could go wrong: $11.5B market cap on $235M midpoint revenue = 49x EV/Sales. Wolfpack short report alleging $54.6M revenue black hole from lost Pentagon contracts remains unresolved. SkyWater acquisition adds $1.8B integration risk. Burning $320M+/year despite revenue growth.
IonQ Reports FY2025 Results: Revenue Up 202%, Guides 2026 at $225-245M

Beat guidance by 20%, achieved 99.99% two-qubit gate fidelity. RPO surged to $370M from $77M.

IonQ Press Release

IonQ Acquires SkyWater Technology for $1.8B to Create Vertically Integrated Quantum Platform

Strategic acquisition of chip manufacturer for $15/share cash + stock. Creates domestic quantum computing supply chain. Closing Q2-Q3 2026.

Manufacturing Dive

IonQ Selected for $151B MDA SHIELD IDIQ Contract

Missile Defense Agency framework contract for quantum computing in defense applications. Indefinite delivery/quantity — IonQ competes for task orders.

IonQ Newsroom

Wolfpack Research Alleges $54.6M Black Hole from Lost Government Earmarks

Short seller claims Pentagon funding missing from federal budget for second consecutive year. IonQ disputes claims.

Data Center Dynamics

IonQ Stock Rally Questions: Too Late After 198% Gain?

Market commentary questions valuation after massive run-up. Stock has since pulled back 24% from peak.

Intuitive Machines
LUNR 58/100 Space / Lunar
$17.83
-$1.08 (-5.71%)
Mkt Cap: $2.5B
Cash: ~$757M estimated (post-Lanteris: $582.6M + $175M strategic raise Feb 2026)
Burn Rate: $106.8M net loss FY2025 (larger than prior $83.3M estimate). Free cash flow -$56M FY2025
Timing: This quarter — Q1 2026 earnings (likely May/June) will be first test of $900M-$1B guidance credibility. LTV contract task orders could drive near-term sentiment. Lanteris integration milestones critical.
What Changed: Net loss wider at $106.8M (vs prior $83.3M estimate). Lanteris acquisition closed for $800M (Jan 2026) — cash position significantly reduced. $175M strategic raise at $15.12/share. CEO insider selling ESCALATED to 6M shares (up from 3.1M in prior data). NASA LTV formally awarded. Analyst upgrades from KeyBanc/Canaccord.
Bull case: Guiding 4-5x revenue growth to $900M-$1B with ~67% already contracted ($943M backlog). NASA LTV Moon RACER team selected — potential for $1.5B+ in awards. Lanteris acquisition transforms into full-spectrum space prime contractor with vertically integrated manufacturing. Targeting positive Adjusted EBITDA in 2026. Only $2.5B market cap vs $950M midpoint revenue = 2.6x EV/Sales.
What could go wrong: Cash position dropped dramatically after $800M Lanteris acquisition ($450M cash component). CEO sold 6M shares in 90 days — most aggressive insider selling on watchlist. 5x revenue jump ($210M → $950M) requires flawless execution on multiple new programs. Integration risk on Lanteris + KinetX acquisitions is high. $175M raise at $15.12/share was below market, signaling capital need.
Intuitive Machines Reports Q4 2025: $210M Revenue, Guides $900M-$1B for 2026

FY2025 revenue slightly above guidance. Net loss $106.8M. Bold 4-5x growth guidance with 67% already contracted.

Intuitive Machines Closes $800M Lanteris Space Systems Acquisition

Paid $450M cash + $350M stock for satellite manufacturing capability. Vertically integrates LUNR's space platform.

Bloom Energy
BE 57/100 Clean Energy
$150.12
-$16.57 (-9.94%)
Mkt Cap: $42.1B
Cash: $2.5B (end of 2025)
Burn Rate: Positive operating cash flow $418.1M in 2025; cash generative
Timing: This quarter — Q1 2026 earnings (April) will validate margin/guidance. Capacity doubling to 2GW by EOY 2026 is operational milestone. Oracle partnership execution and Brookfield deployment ramp are near-term catalysts.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Exceptional revenue growth trajectory: $2.02B (2025) to $3.1-3.3B guidance (2026), 53-63% YoY increase. $20B total backlog ($6B product + $14B service) provides multi-year visibility. Positive OCF $418M in 2025 and AI data center demand validate market opportunity.
What could go wrong: Massive insider selling: $34.7M in last 90 days including CEO/CLO/CCO raises governance concerns. Oracle/OpenAI Texas data center cancellation removes near-term revenue catalyst. Stock up 80% YTD creates valuation risk; guidance assumes flawless execution at 2GW capacity doubling target.
Bloom Energy Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Results with Record Full Year Revenues

Record $2.02B revenue in 2025 (up 37.3% YoY). 2026 guidance raised to $3.1-3.3B. Q4 EPS of $0.45 beat expectations.

Bloom Energy IR

Oracle and OpenAI Cancel Plans: Bloom Energy Shares Fall

Texas data center project cancellation removes significant revenue opportunity. Stock down 2.1% on the news.

Data Centers Plan to Reduce Reliance on Grid Finds Bloom Energy's 2026 Power Report

Survey shows data center leaders investing in onsite power for scaling operations. Validates Bloom's core market thesis.

Bloom Energy CLO Soderberg Sells $6.5M in Stock

CLO sold 42,881 shares March 16-17 at ~$150.47/share under Rule 10b5-1 pre-arranged plan adopted November 2025.

Investing.com

Bloom Energy Jumps as Bold 2026 Outlook Ignites Rally

Raised 2026 revenue guidance well above Wall Street consensus; stock rallied 80% YTD on backlog strength and AI data center opportunity.

D-Wave Quantum
QBTS 56/100 Quantum Computing
$15.73
-$0.37 (-2.30%)
Mkt Cap: $5.8B
Cash: $884.5M cash and marketable securities (+397% YoY)
Burn Rate: ~$100M annualized (estimated from adj. net loss of $84.5M)
Timing: This quarter — Need to see if $30M+ January bookings momentum sustains through Q1 2026. Government business unit contract wins will be key. QCI integration and gate-model development milestones expected throughout 2026.
What Changed: Cash position increased from $836M to $884.5M. Revenue disclosed at $24.6M (+179% YoY). January 2026 bookings >$30M vs prior note of '$30M+ bookings' — now confirmed at >$32.8M YTD as of Feb 25. New government business unit launched. QCI acquisition completed. Price declined from $18.83 to $15.73 (-16.5% over 20 sessions). Insider selling increased modestly.
Bull case: D-Wave is the quantum revenue leader with $24.6M (+179% YoY) and $884.5M cash providing 8+ years of runway. January 2026 bookings of $30M+ exceeded all of FY2025 — commercial traction is real and accelerating. QCI acquisition adds gate-model capability, creating a dual-platform quantum company. Government business unit and defense partnerships (Davidson Tech, Anduril) open a massive TAM. 82.6% gross margins show strong pricing power.
What could go wrong: $5.8B market cap on $24.6M revenue = ~236x trailing sales — extreme valuation requires continued hypergrowth. $355M net loss (including $250M non-cash charges) shows profitability is years away. QCI acquisition ($550M) is a big bet on unproven gate-model technology. Quantum computing commercial viability timeline remains uncertain. Stock pulled back ~13% over 20 sessions as quantum hype cools.
D-Wave Reports Fourth Quarter and Year-End 2025 Results

Revenue $24.6M (+179% YoY). Cash position $884.5M. January 2026 bookings >$30M exceeded all of FY2025. Gross margin expanded to 82.6%.

D-Wave IR

D-Wave Announces Agreement to Acquire Quantum Circuits Inc.

D-Wave acquired QCI for $550M ($300M stock + $250M cash), adding gate-model quantum computing to create world's first dual-platform quantum company.

D-Wave Quantum Expands Dual-Platform Ambitions With New Contracts And U.S. Hub

New Boca Raton HQ and R&D hub, plus new contracts and government business unit, signal aggressive U.S. expansion.

Is D-Wave Quantum Targeting a Durable Edge With Its New U.S. Government Unit?

Analysis of D-Wave's new government business unit under Jack Sears Jr. and potential for federal quantum computing contract wins.

Symbotic Inc.
SYM 55/100 AI / Automation
$50.12
-$1.16 (-2.26%)
Mkt Cap: $30.2B
Cash: $1.8B cash (including $574M sequential increase from follow-on offering)
Burn Rate: GAAP profitable — $13M net income in Q1 FY2026
Timing: This quarter — Q2 FY2026 earnings expected in May 2026. Watch for sequential revenue ramp and margin expansion as guided.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: First-ever GAAP profitability ($13M net income) proves the unit economics work at scale. $22.3B backlog provides extraordinary revenue visibility for years ahead. Software revenue growing 97% YoY is the higher-margin growth lever. $1.8B cash means zero financing risk. Warehouse automation is a secular trend driven by labor shortages and e-commerce.
What could go wrong: At $30.2B market cap with ~$2.6B estimated FY2026 revenue, trades at ~12x sales — rich for a hardware-heavy company. Revenue growth decelerated to 29% in Q1 from higher prior rates. Multiple insiders selling (though under 10b5-1 plans). Heavy reliance on Walmart as a customer (concentration risk). Supply chain and execution risk on $22.3B backlog — any delays could hurt margins.
Symbotic Q1 2026 revenue hits $630M, swings to profit

First-ever GAAP profitability with $13M net income and $67M adj. EBITDA. Revenue up 29% YoY with software surging 97%.

StockTitan

How Investors Are Reacting To Symbotic (SYM) Earnings Beat, Analyst Upgrades, And Insider Share Sale

Strong Q1 results drove analyst upgrades, though insider selling created mixed sentiment among investors.

Yahoo Finance

Symbotic Named One of New England's Fastest-Growing Companies by the Boston Globe

Ranked #9 on Boston Globe's growth list, highlighting rapid revenue expansion in warehouse automation.

StockTitan

Symbotic Stock Surges on Automation-Driven Revenue Growth. Is SYM a Buy Now for 2026?

Analysis of whether SYM's strong backlog and profitability inflection justify current valuation.

Beam Therapeutics
BEAM 55/100 Biotech
$24.63
-$0.54 (-2.15%)
Mkt Cap: $2.5B
Cash: $1.25B (cash, equivalents, and marketable securities at year-end 2025) + $100M debt facility with up to $400M more available
Burn Rate: Data unavailable for precise 2026 OpEx guidance; historical EPS of -$0.81 annualized suggests manageable burn
Timing: This quarter — BEAM-302 Phase 1/2 data expected by end of Q1 2026 (imminent). Risto-cel BLA submission by year-end 2026. Two significant catalysts this year make BEAM a high-event-density stock.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Risto-cel BLA submission by year-end 2026 for SCD with remarkable Phase 1/2 data (>60% HbF, anemia resolution, no severe VOCs). $1.25B cash + $500M debt facility provides runway through commercial transition to mid-2029. Base editing platform differentiates from CRISPR (no double-strand breaks, potentially safer). Analyst consensus target $47.50 implies 93% upside.
What could go wrong: Stock has declined from $28.33 to $24.63 (-13%) over 20 days, reflecting broader biotech selloff. Insider selling of $88.7M over time with zero insider buying is concerning. SCD competitive landscape includes CRISPR/Vertex's Casgevy (already approved). Manufacturing scale-up for commercial launch is unproven. BEAM-302 data readout in Q1 could disappoint.
Beam Therapeutics To Report BEAM-302 Trial Data In Q1; Submit Risto-cel BLA By Year-End 2026

Two major 2026 milestones: BEAM-302 Phase 1/2 data imminent and risto-cel BLA submission planned for year-end.

Nasdaq

Beam Therapeutics Reports Updated Data from BEACON Phase 1/2 Trial Highlighting Durable, Differentiated Profile in Sickle Cell Disease

31 SCD patients show >60% HbF induction, HbS <40%, anemia resolution up to 20 months. No severe VOCs post-engraftment.

Beam Therapeutics IR

Beam Therapeutics Sets Strategic Priorities and Extends Operating Runway through Commercial Transition

Strategic prioritization focuses on risto-cel commercialization and genetic disease franchise; $100M debt facility secured with $400M additional capacity.

GlobeNewsWire

Beam Therapeutics Inc. Receives $47.50 Average Target Price from Analysts

Analyst consensus target of $47.50 implies 93% upside from current price; reflects confidence in risto-cel and BEAM-302 catalysts.

-$0.01 (-0.62%)
Mkt Cap: $347M
Cash: Data unavailable — specific cash position not disclosed in search results; estimated 12-18 months runway based on burn rate
Burn Rate: Deeply unprofitable — EPS -$1.22, net margin -323.12%
Timing: This week — Q4/FY2025 earnings call on March 31 (8 days) will provide 2026 guidance and strategic alternatives update. This is the key near-term catalyst.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Revenue inflection is real — $81.7M TTM with 84.8% 3-year CAGR and Q4 beat above guidance. Strategic alternatives review with Guggenheim/Houlihan Lokey could unlock M&A premium. Institutional accumulation from ARK (+23.6%) and Mitsubishi UFJ (+96.4%) signals smart money confidence. Poison pill signals takeover interest. Redomestication to US improves corporate governance optics. Options surge suggests bullish positioning. If industrial 3D-printed PCB adoption accelerates (defense, aerospace, medical devices), revenue scales 5-10x.
What could go wrong: Deeply unprofitable at -323% net margin with unclear path to profitability. Estimated 12-18 months cash runway means dilutive raise likely in 2026-2027. Poison pill adopted suggests governance concerns and potential hostile activism. Stock down 20% from $2.00 to $1.59 in persistent downtrend. Analyst targets wildly divergent ($0.17-$14.47) reflecting extreme uncertainty. 3D-printed PCB adoption remains slow in commercial electronics. Competition from traditional subtractive manufacturing on cost basis.
Nano Dimension Adopts Limited Duration Shareholder Rights Agreement

Board implements 9.99% poison pill after Oramed Pharma builds 5% stake — signals hostile takeover defense and potential activism.

GlobeNewsWire

Nano Dimension to Host Q4 and Full-Year 2025 Financial Results Conference Call on March 31

Earnings call will provide 2026 outlook and update on strategic alternatives review with Guggenheim/Houlihan Lokey.

GlobeNewsWire

Nano Dimension Expects Higher Q4 Revenue, Surpasses Guidance

Q4 revenue expected $35.0-35.5M vs guidance of $31.5-33.5M — execution improving as 3D-printed electronics gain traction.

GuruFocus

ARK Investment Management Boosts Nano Dimension Position by 23.6%

Cathie Wood's ARK added 339K shares, now holding 1.78M shares worth $2.88M — institutional confidence signal.

Camtek Ltd.
CAMT 54/100 Semiconductors
$165.95
-$6.30 (-3.66%)
Mkt Cap: $7.6B
Cash: Profitable — FY2025 non-GAAP net income $159.0M (+15% YoY)
Burn Rate: N/A — profitable (GAAP net income $50.7M, non-GAAP $159.0M FY2025)
Timing: 6+ months — 2026 growth weighted to H2. No imminent binary catalyst. Semiconductor capex cycle needs to hold through 2026 for bull thesis to play out. Next major catalyst likely Q2 2026 earnings showing order backlog conversion.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Positioned at the epicenter of AI-driven fab capacity expansion. $45M Hawk Systems orders from Tier-1 IDM validates advanced AI chip inspection demand. Revenue $496.1M (+16%) with double-digit growth guided for 2026. Non-GAAP net income $159M shows strong profitability. Multiple analyst upgrades: Oppenheimer $180, Stifel $185 (8-15% upside from $165.95). Advanced packaging and AI wafer inspection are structural growth drivers as leading manufacturers aggressively expand capacity.
What could go wrong: Analyst target divergence ($110-185) reflects peak-cycle debate — if fab capex slows in H2 2026, stock retreats to $120-130. GAAP net income dropped 43% YoY ($50.7M) despite revenue growth, suggesting one-time charges or margin pressure. H2-weighted 2026 guidance means front-half weakness possible. Small-cap equipment maker ($165.95 stock) faces cyclicality risk inherent in semiconductor capex cycles. Modest insider selling in Feb 2026.
Camtek Secures $45 Million in Hawk System Orders for AI-Focused IDM Deliveries in 2026

Tier-1 IDM placed $25M initial + follow-on orders for Hawk inspection systems targeting AI chip manufacturing.

Camtek IR

Camtek Announces Record Results for Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025

Revenue $496.1M (+16%), non-GAAP EPS $3.26. CEO calls company 'at the epicenter of the AI market'.

Camtek IR

Oppenheimer, Morgan Stanley, Stifel Raise Camtek Price Targets

Post-earnings upgrades: Oppenheimer to $180, Morgan Stanley to $151, Stifel to $185 on AI inspection demand.

Multiple Analysts

Camtek Investor Presentation Highlights Advanced Packaging Growth

January 2026 presentation positioned advanced packaging as key 2026 growth anchor alongside AI-driven inspection demand.

Tempus AI
TEM 53/100 Digital Health
$46.96
-$1.78 (-3.65%)
Mkt Cap: $8.4B
Cash: $759.7M cash and marketable securities (Dec 2025)
Burn Rate: $218.1M annual operating cash burn (2025); improving — achieved adj. EBITDA positive in Q3 2025
Timing: This quarter — Merck partnership progress and Q1 2026 earnings (likely May) will test revenue trajectory. ADLT/MRD reimbursement developments could shift unit economics. No single binary catalyst imminent.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Tempus is the AI diagnostics platform leader with 83% revenue growth, partnerships with 95% of top 20 pharma companies including new Merck multi-year deal. Revenue of $1.27B scaling to $1.59B in 2026. Achieved adj. EBITDA positive — profitability inflection underway. $759.7M cash provides 3.5yr runway. 87% customer retention among physicians ordering >5 NGS tests. 50%+ of US oncologists connected. ADLT/MRD reimbursement unlocking regulatory pricing premium. Analyst avg PT $79.45 (+69% upside).
What could go wrong: Still GAAP unprofitable with $218M annual cash burn and elevated debt-to-equity ratio of 15.44. CEO selling >$16M in stock with zero insider buying. Stock down 18% from $57 to $47 despite raising 2026 guidance — valuation compression. True FCF positive likely not until 2027+. Digital health/AI diagnostics face reimbursement uncertainty. Competition from Guardant Health, Foundation Medicine. ADLT migration pricing uplift not guaranteed.
Tempus AI and Merck Enter Multi-Year Strategic Collaboration

Partnership accelerates AI-driven precision medicine in oncology, validating Tempus's data platform and pharma value proposition.

Tempus IR

Comprehensive Genomic Profiling Uncovers 12% of Patients with Actionable Findings Missed by Limited Testing

JCO Precision Oncology study validates Tempus's comprehensive testing approach over limited panels.

JCO Precision Oncology

Tempus AI Revenue Soars 83%, Reaches Profitability Milestone and Issues Strong 2026 Outlook

FY2025 revenue $1.27B, achieved adj. EBITDA positive. 2026 guidance $1.59B but stock sold off 6.9% on announcement.

SignalBloom

Illumina and Tempus Partner to Drive the Future of Precision Medicine Through Genomic AI Innovation

Combines Illumina AI with Tempus's multimodal clinical data for next-gen genomic algorithm development.

MP Materials
MP 53/100 Mining / Rare Earths
$50.60
-$2.50 (-4.71%)
Mkt Cap: $9.0B
Cash: $1.83B total cash (as of Feb 27, 2026)
Burn Rate: $328.1M negative free cash flow FY2025 (heavy CapEx for rare earth processing buildout)
Timing: This quarter to 6 months — NdPr metal production ramp at Independence is the key catalyst. Next earnings May 7, 2026. Government rare earth policy developments could accelerate timeline.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: MP is the only scaled rare earth producer in the Western Hemisphere with $1.83B cash providing 5+ years of runway. Record production of 50,692 metric tons (+12% YoY) shows operational execution. NdPr processing at Independence is the key value unlock — moving from concentrate to separated oxides and metals commands 3-5x price premiums. Geopolitical imperative (China controls 60%+ of processing) creates bipartisan policy tailwind. Analyst consensus Buy with $74.64-$79.50 target (48-57% upside).
What could go wrong: CEO sold $59.3M in stock — the largest insider selling of any ticker in this batch. Free cash flow deeply negative at -$328M as heavy CapEx continues. Revenue of $224M growing only 10% doesn't justify $9B market cap. USA Rare Earth emergence as government-backed competitor threatens monopoly position. Rare earth prices remain depressed from Chinese oversupply. Next earnings May 7 with only $0.01 EPS expected.
Major Investors Are Buying Up Shares of MP Materials. Here's Why the Industrial Stock Could Soar in 2026 and Beyond.

Institutional investors adding positions ahead of Independence processing ramp. Geopolitical rare earth supply chain thesis gaining momentum.

Motley Fool

USA Rare Earth vs. MP Materials: Which Mining Stock Should You Buy in 2026?

New government-backed competitor USA Rare Earth challenges MP's monopoly. MP has scale advantage but USA Rare Earth has political backing.

Motley Fool

Where Will MP Materials Stock Be in 5 Years?

Long-term thesis intact with Independence processing ramp, but near-term profitability remains elusive with $328M FCF burn.

Motley Fool

MP Materials slides as investors weigh new U.S.-backed rival and shifting rare-earth funding landscape

Stock declined on fears that government funding may shift to competitors. USA Rare Earth DOE backing creates uncertainty for MP's competitive position.

Quiver Quantitative

Intellia Therapeutics
NTLA 52/100 Biotech
$12.84
-$0.31 (-2.36%)
Mkt Cap: $1.5B
Cash: $605.1M (cash, equivalents, and marketable securities at year-end 2025)
Burn Rate: ~$400-500M annualized (estimated from analyst FY2026 EPS forecast of -$5.07 × ~100M shares)
Timing: 6+ months — MAGNITUDE Phase 3 enrollment restart will take time. Mid-2026 Phase 3 lonvo-z (HAE) readout is nearest catalyst. BLA possible H2 2026 for HAE but Phase 3 ATTR-CM data is years away. Near-term, stock may stabilize post-hold-lift.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: FDA lifting clinical holds on both MAGNITUDE and MAGNITUDE-2 validates nex-z safety profile with mitigations. $605M cash provides 18-month runway. In vivo CRISPR gene editing for ATTR is a massive market ($5B+ addressable). Director's $1.4M open-market purchase is a strong bullish signal. Q4 revenue beat of 89% shows collaboration momentum.
What could go wrong: Patient death triggered original clinical hold (Grade 4 liver toxicity) — safety overhang persists. Burn rate of $400-500M/year means cash runway is only 15-18 months, likely needing capital raise. Stock down from $15.44 peak to $12.84 (-17%) in 20 days. CEO selling while director buying sends mixed signals. MAGNITUDE enrollment of 1,200 patients is a massive, costly undertaking.
Intellia Therapeutics Announces FDA Lift of Clinical Hold on MAGNITUDE Phase 3 Clinical Trial in ATTR-CM

FDA removes clinical hold after implementation of safety mitigations including intensified liver monitoring and steroid guidance. Trial can resume enrollment.

Intellia Therapeutics IR

Intellia Therapeutics Shares Surge Amid Stellar Earnings and Promising CRISPR Advances

Q4 2025 EPS of -$0.83 beats by $0.16; revenue of $23M up 78% YoY. Clinical hold lift drives optimism.

Intellia Therapeutics Announces FDA Lift of Clinical Hold on MAGNITUDE-2 Phase 3 in ATTRv-PN

Earlier MAGNITUDE-2 hold lifted January 27; both Phase 3 programs now cleared to resume enrollment.

Intellia Therapeutics IR

Intellia Leadership Shakes Up Insider Holdings With Major Buy Amid Executive Sell-Off

Director Fred Cohen bought 150,000 shares ($1.4M) while CEO sold 34,146 shares — mixed signals from insiders.

PolyPid Ltd.
PYPD 52/100 Biotech / MedTech
$4.16
-$0.21 (-4.81%)
Mkt Cap: $79M
Cash: $18.8M (up from $15.6M at 2024 year-end)
Burn Rate: ~$12M annualized (estimated operating cash outflow)
Timing: This week / This month — Rolling NDA submission due by end of March 2026. Partnership announcement could come any time given 'advanced' talks. If both NDA filing and partnership land in March, this is a near-term catalyst-rich setup.
What Changed: Cash position improved from $16.6M to $18.8M (warrant exercises). PDUFA fee waiver received March 17 ($4.3M savings). Price declined from $4.56 to $4.16 (-8.8% over past 20 sessions). NDA submission imminent but not yet filed. Partnership still pending.
Bull case: D-PLEX100 cleared Phase 3 with a statistically significant 60% reduction in surgical site infections (p=0.0013), and FDA pre-NDA meeting confirmed data package is adequate for NDA review. $4.3M PDUFA fee waiver saves ~25% of cash, and advanced U.S. partnership talks could bring non-dilutive funding. Analyst target $12.50 implies ~3x upside from $4.16.
What could go wrong: Only $18.8M cash with ~$12M annual burn gives roughly 18 months runway — survival into 2027 approval window is uncertain without a partnership deal. NDA review takes 10-12 months, so even if filed now, approval is mid-2027 at earliest. Micro-cap ($79M) with only 44K daily volume means limited liquidity and high volatility risk.
PolyPid Receives PDUFA Fee Waiver from FDA for D-PLEX₁₀₀'s New Drug Application

FDA waived the $4.3M PDUFA user fee, preserving cash for the NDA filing and commercialization prep. Rolling NDA on track for end of March 2026.

GlobeNewsWire

PolyPid Announces Positive FDA Pre-NDA Meeting Minutes for D-PLEX₁₀₀ Supporting NDA Submission

FDA agreed existing clinical data from SHIELD II Phase 3 appears adequate for NDA, supporting rolling submission beginning early 2026.

PolyPid IR

PolyPid targets rolling NDA submission for D-PLEX100 by end of Q1 2026 amid advanced U.S. partnership talks

Management confirmed NDA filing timeline and disclosed advanced discussions with potential U.S. commercial partners for hospital-focused distribution.

Seeking Alpha

PolyPid Earnings Call Flags Big 2026 Catalyst

Q4 2025 earnings call highlighted SHIELD II success and 2026 NDA filing as the critical catalyst for value creation.

UEC 52/100 Mining / Uranium
$12.09
-$1.19 (-8.96%)
Mkt Cap: $5.9B
Cash: $818M liquid assets including $486M cash (Q2 2026)
Burn Rate: Profitable on uranium sales; net loss -$13.94M Q2 2026 includes non-cash items
Timing: This quarter — Burke Hollow startup ongoing; full capacity ramp 2-3 quarters away. Regulatory approval delays are near-term headwinds. Next catalyst: Q3 2026 production guidance and ISR header house approvals.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: UEC is lowest-cost uranium producer at $39.66/lb with 1.456M lbs U3O8 inventory valued $144M. Q2 2026 achieved $101/lb premium pricing. Burke Hollow ISR online adds capacity. $818M cash provides 25+ year runway. T. Rowe Price added 42.3M shares (+247%) signaling institutional confidence.
What could go wrong: Q2 2026 net loss -$13.94M despite $20M revenue suggests high capex/depreciation burden. Production guidance down Q2 due to regulatory delays in header house approvals and Burke Hollow startup issues. Premium $101/lb may not sustain; breakeven ~$75-80/lb. Stock down 25% from January highs.
Q2 2026: UEC achieved $101/lb pricing on 200,000 lbs U3O8 sales; $818M liquid assets

Revenue $20.2M, net loss -$13.94M. Production 45,743 lbs at $39.66/lb cash cost. Uranium inventory 1.456M lbs valued $144M.

SEC Filings / Uranium Energy

Burke Hollow ISR uranium mine completed; newest U.S. facility online

Burke Hollow completion marks newest ISR uranium facility in U.S. Production ramp ongoing but regulatory delays affecting header house startups.

Uranium Energy Q2 Earnings

Institutional buying surge: Price T Rowe Associates added 42.3M shares (+247%)

Major institutional investor added $563.7M in UEC shares, signaling confidence in uranium demand and low-cost positioning.

Ambarella Inc.
AMBA 52/100 Semiconductors
$54.02
-$1.84 (-3.29%)
Mkt Cap: $2.3B
Cash: $312.6M cash and marketable securities (Q4 FY2026)
Burn Rate: N/A — positive operating cash flow ($73.5M FY2026); GAAP net loss -$75.9M driven by R&D/SBC
Timing: This quarter — Q1 FY2027 earnings ~May 2026 will reveal whether CV7 design wins are translating to orders. DRAM cost headwinds expected H1 2026. Edge AI demand cycle is 2-3 year secular play.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Pure-play edge AI vision processor leader riding 17-21% CAGR market ($30.7B in 2026 → $68.7B by 2031). CV7 on 4nm is best-in-class with 2.5x AI performance improvement. 370 customer projects in production with 80% revenue from AI solutions. $312.6M cash with positive OCF. Automotive ADAS represents 35% of vision chip demand with early-innings adoption. Record FY2026 revenue $390.7M (+37%). Analyst consensus Strong Buy with avg PT $97 (+80% upside).
What could go wrong: CEO/COO/VPs sold $2M+ in coordinated selling at $52-54 despite $97 analyst targets — suggests management knows near-term headwinds. Stock down 20% from $67 to $54 despite earnings beat — market pricing in growth deceleration. Q1 FY2027 guidance flat/down QoQ signals demand softness. DRAM prices expected to surge 55-60% in Q1 2026, compressing margins. China export control risk for surveillance/IoT customers (not quantified). Small scale ($390M revenue) vs. Qualcomm ($39B+) limits R&D firepower.
Ambarella Launches Powerful Edge AI 8K Vision SoC with Industry-Leading AI and Multi-Sensor Perception Performance

CV7 on 4nm delivers 2.5x AI performance and 20% lower power than CV5. Targets 8K cameras, robotics, automotive, security.

Ambarella IR

Ambarella Q4 FY2026 Earnings Beat — Record Revenue Year

FY2026 revenue $390.7M (+37%), non-GAAP EPS $0.62. IoT segment grew ~50% YoY driving outperformance.

Ambarella IR

Insider Selling: Ambarella CEO Sells $437K in Stock

CEO Feng-Ming Wang sold 8,083 shares at $54.14 on March 19, adding to $1M sale two days prior. Part of coordinated C-suite selling.

Ambarella Launches DevZone to Accelerate AI Development

No-code/low-code platform with Cooper Model Garden and validated AI blueprints. Ecosystem expansion play for design win stickiness.

Timing: This quarter — Q4 results already drove 19% surge. Backlog conversion should begin showing in Q1/Q2 2026 revenue. AFRL HANGTIME and Navy missile milestones expected throughout 2026. Revenue inflection is the key test.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Revenue guidance of $24-27M represents 357-440% growth with $18M backlog (up from $13.5M in ~2 months). AFRL HANGTIME and Navy missile contracts validate SwarmOS platform across multiple domains. CEO buying shares signals insider confidence. Defense spending trends favor autonomous systems/AI. Q4 revenue doubled YoY.
What could go wrong: $47M cash provides only 15-18 months runway at current burn. FY2025 revenue actually declined 33% to $5.2M despite contract wins. $295M market cap on $5.2M revenue (57x sales) requires massive growth execution. Backlog conversion to revenue is unproven at scale. Defense contract timelines are unpredictable.
Palladyne AI Shares Surge 19% on Impressive Q4 2025 Results

Q4 revenue of $1.7M doubled YoY. Strong backlog growth and defense contract wins drove market enthusiasm.

Market Reports

AFRL Awards Palladyne AI HANGTIME Contract for Swarming Autonomous Systems

Air Force Research Laboratory contract to integrate SwarmOS platform across space, air, maritime, and land domains.

Palladyne AI IR

GuideTech Awarded US Navy Contract for Near-Hypersonic Missile Development

Palladyne subsidiary developing air-launched, long-range, low-cost missile. Validates defense technology platform.

BusinessWire

Palladyne AI Names Matt Muta as President, Commercial and Industrial

New hire to accelerate enterprise AI platform deployment beyond defense into commercial markets.
