Ur-Energy Inc.
URG 51/100 Mining / Uranium
$1.45
+$0.13 (+9.85%)
Mkt Cap: $576M
Cash: $123.9M (Dec 31, 2025); $115.3M (March 4, 2026 pre-warrant exercise)
Burn Rate: Operating loss ~$17.97M annually (pre-production ramp)
Timing: This month — Shirley Basin startup Q1 2026 is imminent (within weeks). Binary catalyst: success = $30M+ annual FCF generation and stock re-rate; delay = liquidity crisis by Q2-Q3 2026.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Shirley Basin startup Q1 2026 is imminent catalyst to eliminate $17.97M annual operating loss. Cost structure 75% lower than Lost Creek; breakeven ~$40/lb at 800K-1.2M lb production. 1.3M lbs contracted sales through 2033 at $82M value provides 5+ years visibility. Warrant exercise ($18.5M expected) adds runway cushion.
What could go wrong: Heavy insider selling (7 sales vs 2 buys over 6 months) raises concerns. Shirley Basin startup delay could trigger liquidity crisis. Operating loss $17.97M annually unsustainable beyond 12-14 months without production revenue. CEO transition Dec 2025 adds execution risk.
Ur-Energy Q4 2025 results: 410,440 lbs drummed U3O8 (+65% YoY); Shirley Basin ready Q1 2026

Production up 65% on Lost Creek ramp. Cash $123.9M at Dec 31. Shirley Basin construction advanced; startup expected Q1 2026 with 75% cost reduction vs Lost Creek.

Ur-Energy

Ur-Energy secures 1.3M lbs uranium contracts through 2033 (~$82M value)

8 uranium sales agreements locked in multi-year contracts. Provides 5+ year revenue visibility and de-risks demand.

Ur-Energy Press Releases

Insider buying activity drives URG stock up 8% on March 20, 2026

After recent 13% selloff, insider buying triggered recovery. Net 9 trades in 6 months (2 buys, 7 sells) — mixed insider sentiment but recent buys may signal confidence in Shirley Basin.

Rocket Lab USA
RKLB 51/100 Space / Launch
$67.23
-$4.70 (-6.53%)
Mkt Cap: $38.1B
Cash: $1.1B (Q4 2025; up from $271M YE2024 via ATM facility)
Burn Rate: $456M annualized negative FCF; Q1 2026 expected peak Neutron R&D spending
Timing: This quarter to Q4 2026 — Neutron first launch remains the transformational catalyst. Each Electron mission (cadence accelerating) validates operations. Q1 2026 earnings will test revenue guidance credibility. SpaceX IPO buzz could lift sector.
What Changed: Cash position confirmed at $1.1B (up from $271M, stronger than implied). FY2026 guidance of $885M is NEW. Backlog exceeds $2B (up from $1.85B launch-only). Optical Support acquisition is NEW M&A. Insider selling -$13.6M is NEW bearish signal. Stock down 6.53% today despite recent contract wins.
Bull case: Second most prolific orbital launcher after SpaceX with 84 Electron launches and 100% 2025 success rate. $2B+ total backlog (73% YoY growth). 28 new launches sold in Q1 2026 alone shows demand acceleration. $190M HASTE + $816M SDA contracts cement defense position. $885M revenue guidance implies 47% growth.
What could go wrong: Neutron delayed 9 months to Q4 2026 after tank test failure — first revenue likely 2027. Peak R&D spending in Q1 2026 pressures cash burn. $38.1B market cap on $885M guided revenue (43x sales) prices in perfection. -$13.6M insider selling amid 67% rally is bearish divergence. $1.1B cash with $456M annual burn = 2.4 years if no ATM.
Rocket Lab Secures $190M HASTE Contract for 20 Hypersonic Test Launches

Largest single launch contract in company history. Cements hypersonics testing leadership with Department of Defense partnership.

GlobeNewswire

Successful 'Eight Days A Week' Electron Mission #84 for Synspective

Maintained 100% mission success rate. Demonstrates operational cadence reliability for growing customer base.

Rocket Lab Newsroom

Rocket Lab Stock Jumps 6.44% on Record Q4 Earnings and 73% Backlog Growth

Revenue beat expectations. Backlog grew to $1.85B+ (launch) / $2B+ (total including space systems). 28 new launches sold in Q1.

StocksToTrade

Rocket Lab Acquires Optical Support Inc. for Defense Supply Chain Security

20 team members and 22K sq ft facilities acquired to secure national security program supply chain.

Rocket Lab IR

Neutron Tank Test Failure — First Launch Pushed to Q4 2026

Hydrostatic pressure test rupture caused 9-month delay. Spare structure already in build. Fairing and thrust structure qualified.

Energy Fuels Inc.
UUUU 50/100 Mining / Uranium
$16.75
-$1.17 (-6.53%)
Mkt Cap: $4.0B
Cash: $927.4M working capital ($64.7M cash + $797.1M marketable securities + $18M receivables + $73.5M inventory)
Burn Rate: Net cash positive — profitable uranium operations with expanding margins
Timing: This quarter — 2026 production ramp and margin expansion should become visible in Q1/Q2 results. Uranium spot price movements drive near-term sentiment. But insider selling pattern suggests insiders don't expect a near-term pop.
What Changed: Price dropped from $17.92 to $16.75 (-6.53% today, -20% over 20 days). Insider selling pattern continues with 4 sales in March. No new fundamental catalysts since last deep dive. Sparkline shows persistent downtrend from $21+ levels.
Bull case: UUUU is the most diversified US uranium/rare earth producer with $927M working capital, zero debt, and expanding margins. Cost per pound dropping from $43/lb to low $30s vs $70+ spot uranium = massive margin expansion to ~50% in 2026. 2026 production guidance of 2-2.5M lbs represents 45% YoY growth. Dual revenue streams (uranium + rare earths) plus US nuclear energy policy tailwinds.
What could go wrong: Four insider sales in March 2026 totaling $1M+ is a significant yellow flag — insiders are cashing out at current levels. $700M convertible notes create future dilution risk. Uranium spot price remains volatile and geopolitical tensions cut both ways. Stock has fallen 20% in 20 days (from $21 to $16.75), suggesting institutional distribution.
Energy Fuels Announces 2025 Results and 2026 Guidance

Mined 1.72M lbs uranium exceeding guidance, sold 650k lbs for $48.2M. Guiding 2-2.5M lbs production in 2026 with margin expansion to 50%.

PR Newswire

What Could Lift Energy Fuels' Uranium Margins in 2026?

Processing scale-up should drive finished inventory costs from $43/lb to low $30s, dramatically expanding margins vs $70+ uranium spot.

TradingView/Zacks

Energy Fuels VP Sells 25,000 Shares in March 2026 Trade

VP Daniel Kapostasy sold 25k shares at $18.84. Part of broader insider selling pattern — 4 insiders sold $1M+ combined in March.

SEC Form 4

Energy Fuels Q4 2025 Earnings Call Highlights: Strong Uranium Production

Beat production guidance, completed $700M convertible notes offering, working capital swelled to $927M.

Quantum Computing Inc.
QUBT 50/100 Quantum Computing
$6.96
-$0.23 (-3.20%)
Mkt Cap: $1.6B
Cash: $1.52B (year-end 2025; raised $1.64B since Nov 2024)
Burn Rate: $22.2M quarterly operating loss (Q4 2025); masked by $6.97M derivative gains + $13.63M investment income
Timing: 6+ months — Next earnings May 14, 2026. Luminar integration timeline unclear. No near-term catalyst for meaningful revenue generation. Commercial quantum computing viability remains years away.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: $1.52B cash provides 5+ years runway to commercialize photonic quantum technology. Luminar acquisition ($110M) adds photonic chip manufacturing capability. NIST contract validates government interest. Fab 1 facility producing TFLN chips drove 82.8% revenue growth. Ciena partnership for quantum-secure communications shows enterprise interest.
What could go wrong: $1.6B market cap on $682K annual revenue is an extreme valuation disconnect (~2,345x sales). Q4 missed consensus by 50%. Revenue growth is from essentially zero. $1.64B raised since Nov 2024 creates massive dilution risk. Insiders selling while company has negligible revenue.
Quantum Computing Inc. Completes $110M Acquisition of Luminar Semiconductor

All-cash acquisition adds photonic technology patents and engineering team. Accelerates product roadmap for TFLN photonic integrated circuits.

PR Newswire

QUBT Stock Falls 10% After Q4 2025 Revenue Miss

Revenue of $198K missed consensus of $398K by 50%. Net loss narrowed to -$1.56M before derivative gains. Cash position strong at $1.52B.

MarketBeat

Quantum Computing and Ciena Demonstrate Quantum-Secure Communications at OFC 2026

Joint live demo showcasing multilayer quantum-secure architecture. First commercial partnership validation for photonics platform.

Simply Wall St

QUBT CEO Presents at Cantor Global Technology Conference

CEO Dr. Yuping Huang and CFO presented commercial platform strategy to institutional investors in NYC.

SoundHound AI
SOUN 48/100 AI / Voice Tech
$6.55
-$0.32 (-4.73%)
Mkt Cap: $2.8B
Cash: $248M cash, zero debt
Burn Rate: Narrowing — Q4 adj. EBITDA loss $7.4M (improved 56% YoY)
Timing: This quarter — Need to see Q1 2026 execution and CFO transition. Next earnings May 7, 2026 will be critical for validating 2026 guidance trajectory.
What Changed: Price declined from $8.27 to $6.55 (-20.8% over 20 sessions). ManpowerGroup Experis partnership announced March 17. Agentic+ AI launched at GTC. CFO departure confirmed. Revenue guidance reiterated at $225-260M. Prior deep dive noted first entry — this is the update showing continued price weakness despite strong fundamentals.
Bull case: Revenue nearly doubled in 2025 (+99% YoY) with 2026 guidance of $225-260M representing continued strong growth. $248M cash with zero debt provides 3+ years of runway. Agentic AI on-device launch at GTC positions SOUN at edge AI frontier. Enterprise partnerships (Experis, Five Guys, OpenTable) create recurring revenue base. Long-term targets of 70%+ gross margins and 30%+ EBIT margins.
What could go wrong: Stock down ~16% over 20 sessions and 30% YTD despite strong results. CFO departure creates leadership uncertainty. $2.8B market cap on ~$200M trailing revenue = ~14x sales, still richly valued. 25 insider sales with zero purchases in 6 months is a bearish signal. Intense competition from Google, Amazon, Apple in voice AI. Path to profitability remains unclear.
ManpowerGroup's Experis and SoundHound AI Announce Strategic Partnership to Accelerate and Scale Enterprise AI Adoption

SoundHound becomes exclusive conversational AI partner for Experis' new EXCELERATE AI services suite, starting with healthcare sector in the U.S.

GlobeNewsWire

SoundHound AI Targets Autos And Enterprises With New Agentic+ AI Moves

Launched first fully on-device multimodal Agentic+ AI platform for vehicles at NVIDIA GTC 2026, positioning as edge AI leader.

Simply Wall St

SoundHound Extends Partnership with Five Guys, Bolstering AI Presence

AI ordering agents now process over 1 million customer interactions for Five Guys, demonstrating scalable enterprise deployment.

StocksTotrade

SoundHound AI: Adding To My 2026 Watchlist

Analyst argues SoundHound's revenue doubling and strong guidance make it a top AI small-cap, though valuation is stretched.

CRISPR Therapeutics
CRSP 48/100 Biotech
$46.24
-$0.71 (-1.51%)
Mkt Cap: $4.4B
Cash: $1,975.8M (~$2B) cash, equivalents, and marketable securities at year-end 2025
Burn Rate: ~$300-350M annualized net burn (estimated from operating expenses minus Casgevy revenue)
Timing: This quarter / This half — H1 2026 has pediatric Casgevy regulatory submissions and CTX340 trial start. H2 2026 brings CTX310, CTX460, and CTX611 updates. Steady stream of catalysts but no single binary event. Casgevy quarterly revenue trajectory is the key metric to watch.
What Changed: Price dropped from ~$47 to $46.24 (-1.51% today). No material changes from prior deep dive. Casgevy adoption and pipeline milestones on track. CMO insider purchase on Jan 27 is a new positive data point. Broader market selloff continues to pressure stock. Thesis intact — Casgevy ramp is the key variable.
Bull case: CRSP is the only gene editing company with an approved and revenue-generating product (Casgevy: $116M in year one, 300+ patients referred). $2B cash fortress provides 6.5+ year runway — no dilution risk. Deep pipeline: CTX310 (cardiovascular), CTX460 (oncology), CTX611 (autoimmune) all advancing in 2026. CMO insider buying adds confidence.
What could go wrong: Stock has fallen from $61.74 to $46.24 (-25%) in 20 days, reflecting market concerns about Casgevy adoption speed. $4.4B market cap is expensive relative to $116M annual Casgevy revenue (38x sales). Gene therapy manufacturing complexity limits scaling. Competition from Bluebird Bio (Lyfgenia) in SCD and Beam (risto-cel) in pipeline.
CRISPR Therapeutics Highlights Strategic Priorities and Anticipated Milestones for 2026

2026 roadmap includes pediatric Casgevy submissions, CTX310/CTX460/CTX611 clinical updates, and continued commercial acceleration.

Crispr Therapeutics: Prospects For 2026 — Why I Believe Casgevy Is Key

Analysis argues Casgevy adoption trajectory is the single most important variable for CRSP stock in 2026.

Top Executive Makes Bold Move With Fresh Crispr Therapeutics AG Stock Buy

CMO Naimish Patel purchased 1,508 shares ($49.7K) on Jan 27 — insider buying signals personal confidence in company prospects.

CRISPR Therapeutics Advances CASGEVY Access While Shares Trade Below Targets

Casgevy treatment center network expanding; 300+ patient referrals indicate growing demand despite stock trading below analyst targets.

Stem Inc.
STEM 46/100 Clean Energy
$9.70
-$0.76 (-7.27%)
Mkt Cap: $83M
Cash: $48.9M cash end of Q4 2025
Burn Rate: $30M annualized cash cost savings from workforce reduction; targeting path to profitability
Timing: This quarter — Q2 2026 earnings will validate $10-15M adj EBITDA target and ARR path. German projects and water utility deals signal software wins visible in next 60-90 days.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: STEM pivoted to profitability with first full-year positive adjusted EBITDA ($6.7M) and generated $11.4M Q3 OCF. 2026 guidance targets $10-15M adj EBITDA with $65-70M ARR. Software/services revenue grew 25% to $141.4M with 70%+ margins.
What could go wrong: Cash position of $48.9M is tight — CRITICAL: requires 2026 EBITDA to materialize or faces liquidity crisis. Execution risk on $30M cost-savings target and ARR growth to $70M. Competitive pressure from Tesla, FORM, and open-source platforms. Market cap only $83M signals distress.
Stem Reports Q4 2025 Revenue $47.2M, Full Year $156.3M; First Full-Year Positive Adjusted EBITDA $6.7M

Gross margin 38% GAAP; 2026 guidance $140-190M revenue, $10-15M adjusted EBITDA.

Stem's PowerTrack Selected for 90 MWh + 10 MWh German Utility-Scale Battery Storage Projects

Everyray GmbH deployment in Kölsa and Elsterwerda; validates enterprise energy management platform.

CEO Arun Narayanan and Executives Sell STEM Shares in Early March 2026

CEO sold 5,145 shares @ $11.12; routine RSU tax-driven, not conviction signals.

Stem Partners with Southern California Water Utility for 4-Site Battery Storage Portfolio

Hybrid solar+storage integration demonstrates software-centric business model expanding beyond pure battery optimization.

Serve Robotics
SERV 45/100 AI / Robotics
$8.86
-$0.27 (-2.96%)
Mkt Cap: $671M
Cash: $260M cash and marketable securities
Burn Rate: High — $160-170M non-GAAP opex guidance for 2026 + $25M capex
Timing: This quarter — White Castle partnership rollout and Q1 2026 delivery volume data will show if the growth story is accelerating. Watch for additional city launches and restaurant partner announcements.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Revenue growing 10x from $2.7M to $26M guided in 2026 shows rapid scaling. White Castle/Uber Eats partnership expands the addressable restaurant base significantly. 2,000+ robots deployed across 7 cities demonstrates operational capability. Diligent Robotics acquisition opens $500B+ healthcare logistics TAM. Uber relationship provides built-in demand generation platform.
What could go wrong: $260M cash with $170M+ annual burn gives only ~18 months runway — will need additional financing by late 2027. Revenue of $26M doesn't come close to covering costs. Coordinated insider selling by CEO, COO, CFO, and CHO is concerning. Autonomous delivery faces regulatory, weather, and public acceptance hurdles. Diligent Robotics integration risk adds complexity. Competition from Amazon, Nuro, and others.
Serve Robotics and White Castle Launch Autonomous Delivery via Uber Eats

Partnership enables White Castle deliveries via autonomous sidewalk robots on Uber Eats platform. Stock surged 11.1% on the news.

Serve Robotics Announces Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results

FY2025 revenue $2.7M (+46%). 2026 guidance raised to $26M including $7M from Diligent Robotics acquisition. $260M cash position.

Caribou Biosciences
CRBU 45/100 Biotech
$1.87
-$0.10 (-5.08%)
Mkt Cap: $181M
Cash: ~$183.9M (as of Q2 2025; Q4 2025 report shows beat on revenue, exact cash TBD)
Burn Rate: ~$135M annualized (analyst consensus for 2026 net loss of ~$135M)
Timing: 6+ months — Phase 3 trial design and initiation will take time. No imminent binary catalyst. Key inflection is Phase 3 start and financing to fund it. Stock is a longer-duration bet on allogeneic CAR T viability.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Vispa-cel (CB-010) Phase 1 data shows 82% ORR and 64% CR in 2L LBCL — efficacy on par with autologous CAR T therapies but with off-the-shelf convenience. FDA has recommended pivotal Phase 3 path. chRDNA gene editing platform differentiates from CRISPR/Cas9 competitors. 32% workforce cut extends runway and focuses resources.
What could go wrong: Phase 3 trial (~250 patients) will be extremely expensive and cash-intensive — $183M cash may not be sufficient, requiring dilutive financing. $181M market cap vs $135M annual burn means stock is priced for failure. Allogeneic CAR T durability concerns persist (51% 12-month PFS). Competition from Allogene, autologous leaders (BMS, Gilead).
Caribou Biosciences Announces Positive Data from ANTLER Phase 1 Trial Demonstrating Efficacy and Durability of Vispa-cel (CB-010) on Par with Autologous CAR-T Cell Therapies

Vispa-cel shows 82% ORR, 64% CR, and 51% 12-month PFS in 2L LBCL — efficacy comparable to autologous CAR T but off-the-shelf.

Caribou Biosciences IR

Caribou Bio Cuts 32% Staff, Focuses on Key Cancer Programs, Extends Cash to 2027

Strategic restructuring focuses pipeline on CB-010 and CB-011 oncology programs, discontinues lupus trial, extends cash runway by one year.

StockTitan

Caribou Biosciences Posts Earnings Results, Beats Estimates By $0.05 EPS

Q4 2025 EPS of -$0.28 vs consensus -$0.33; revenue of $3.94M vs $1.96M estimate. Better-than-expected performance.

Daily Political

Insider Activity: unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown
Bitfarms Ltd.
BITF 45/100 Crypto Mining
$2.21
-$0.18 (-7.32%)
Mkt Cap: $1.3B
Cash: $87M cash reserves (March 2026)
Burn Rate: Near breakeven (1.3% EBITDA margin); repaid $300M debt facility — now debt-free
Timing: This week — Q4 2025 earnings due March 31, 2026 (8 days). This is a critical report: market will scrutinize HPC/AI pivot progress, Bitcoin mining economics post-halving, and redomiciliation timeline. Binary sentiment shift possible on earnings.
What Changed: Price moved from $2.39 to $2.215 (-7.32% today). Volume spiked to 46M shares (big_move flag). No new fundamental developments since last deep dive. Earnings March 31 remains the imminent catalyst. Net insider buying pattern holds.
Bull case: BITF is transforming from pure Bitcoin miner to diversified HPC/AI data center infrastructure (rebranding as Keel Infrastructure). $192.8M revenue demonstrates operational scale. $300M debt repayment leaves the company debt-free with $87M cash. US redomiciliation opens door to US index inclusion and broader capital access. Net insider buying signals internal confidence in the pivot. ISS recommending FOR redomiciliation vote removes a key uncertainty.
What could go wrong: HPC/AI pivot is unproven — many crypto miners have announced similar pivots but few have successfully executed. EBITDA margin of only 1.3% shows razor-thin profitability that evaporates in a Bitcoin downturn. $87M cash is modest for infrastructure buildout. Bitcoin price correlation remains extremely high regardless of pivot narrative. Rebranding and redomiciling create execution complexity and distraction. Stock volatile at $2.21.
Bitfarms' New Strategy Signifies Digital Infrastructure Leap

Company pivoting from Bitcoin mining to HPC/AI data centers with strategic hires and infrastructure investments.

StocksToTrade

Bitfarms Embraces U.S. Transition Amid Market Growth

ISS recommends shareholders vote FOR US redomiciliation. Rebrand to Keel Infrastructure planned.

TimothySykes

Bitfarms to Rebrand as Keel Infrastructure Amid Major Financial Moves

$300M debt repaid and US redomiciliation plan announced. Strategic shift from crypto mining to digital infrastructure.

TimothySykes

Rigetti Computing
RGTI 44/100 Quantum Computing
$14.88
-$0.53 (-3.44%)
Mkt Cap: $4.9B
Cash: $600M+ (supported by warrant exercises in H2 2025)
Burn Rate: Significant — $600M+ cash provides 3+ years runway
Timing: This quarter — India system delivery in H2 2026. Cepheus-1-108Q commercial traction is the key catalyst. Next earnings expected May 2026. Quantum computing policy tailwinds from White House initiatives could help.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: $600M+ cash with zero debt provides 3+ years of runway for R&D and commercialization. $8.4M India order and $5.8M AFRL contract show international and government demand. 108-qubit Cepheus-1 commercial release is a tangible product milestone. Revenue target of $22.5M in FY2026 would represent massive growth from current ~$10M annual run rate. Modular chiplet architecture could enable scalable quantum systems.
What could go wrong: $4.9B market cap on ~$10M trailing revenue is extremely stretched at ~490x sales. Quantum computing remains largely pre-commercial — most revenue is from R&D systems, not production use. Competition from IBM, Google, D-Wave, IonQ is intense. Stock down ~17% over 20 sessions as quantum sentiment cools. FY2026 $22.5M target is ambitious and depends on lumpy system sales like the India order. Path to profitability is many years away.
Rigetti Computing Secures $8.4M India Quantum Deal

India's Centre for Development of Advanced Computing ordered a 108-qubit system for delivery in H2 2026, validating international demand.

Simply Wall St

Rigetti Computing Announces Strategic Collaboration Agreement with Quanta Computer

Quanta Computer partnership aims to accelerate development and commercialization of superconducting quantum computing systems.

Rigetti IR

Rigetti, in Collaboration with QphoX, Awarded $5.8M AFRL Contract

Three-year Air Force Research Laboratory contract to advance superconducting quantum networking technology.

Rigetti IR

Does Rigetti's $600M Cash Runway De-Risk Its 2026 Roadmap Execution?

Analysis argues strong balance sheet ($600M+ cash, zero debt) provides multi-year visibility for R&D and manufacturing scale-up.

Solid Power
SLDP 42/100 Battery Tech
$3.04
-$0.12 (-3.80%)
Mkt Cap: $671M
Cash: $336.5M total liquidity Dec 2025 + $130M Jan 2026 offering = ~$466M proforma
Burn Rate: $84.5M cash investment FY2025 (ops + CapEx); guidance $85-100M for FY2026
Timing: 6+ months — SK On mass-production 2029 is 2.5+ year inflection. Pilot line commissioning 2026 (positive near-term). First customer revenue unlikely before 2027-2028. High execution risk on manufacturing scale-up.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Solid Power secured $130M capital raise + up to $50M DOE grant, extending runway to 5+ years. SK On accelerated mass-production target to 2029 (positive validation). Samsung SDI and SK On partnerships confirm sulfide-based technology differentiation.
What could go wrong: Only $21.7M revenue FY2025 against $84.5M cash burn — minimal commercial traction. Guidance $85-100M burn for FY2026 vs near-zero revenue suggests commercialization 2-3 years away. High dilution risk from warrant conversions (45.6M shares); $3-3.20/share raise signals weak investor confidence.
Solid Power completes $130M registered direct offering with 17M common shares + 45.6M warrant conversion upside

Single sector-focused institutional investor; extends cash runway significantly.

Solid Power IR

DOE grant amended: up to $50M for electrolyte production capacity expansion

Government support validates sulfide electrolyte production strategy; de-risks scale-up capex.

Solid Power IR

SK On accelerates mass-production target to 2029; Samsung SDI partnership confirms validation

Major OEM commitments validate technology; path to commercial scale clearer.

Solid Power IR

H.C. Wainwright initiates coverage with Buy rating

Analyst support strengthening; price target $7 (2.3x upside from $3.04).

Vanda Pharmaceuticals
VNDA 42/100 Biotech / Pharma
$7.07
-$1.50 (-17.50%)
Mkt Cap: $418M
Cash: $263.8M (cash at year-end 2025)
Burn Rate: Revenue-generating company but reported net loss of $220.5M in FY2025 (includes $113.7M non-cash tax valuation allowance). Adjusted operating loss likely ~$50-75M.
Timing: This quarter / This half — BYSANTI commercial launch Q3 2026 and imsidolimab PDUFA December 12, 2026 are the key catalysts. Near-term, stock may find support after today's 17.5% crash as market digests the guidance. Q1 2026 earnings will be next data point.
What Changed: MAJOR CHANGE: Stock crashed 17.5% today ($8.57 to $7.07) on weak 2026 revenue guidance of $230-260M. This is the biggest single-day move on the entire batch. Prior deep dive data showed stock at higher level. FDA approvals of BYSANTI and NEREUS are new positives. Imsidolimab BLA acceptance with Dec 2026 PDUFA is new catalyst. Revenue guidance disappointment is the dominant narrative today.
Bull case: VNDA has 3 approved and marketed drugs generating $216M revenue plus 2 newly approved products (BYSANTI, NEREUS) launching in 2026. Imsidolimab BLA with December 2026 PDUFA could add 4th growth driver. Fanapt growth of 24% shows strong commercial execution. $263.8M cash provides stability. Historical net insider buying (9 buys vs 2 sells) suggests management confidence. Patent protection on BYSANTI through 2044.
What could go wrong: Stock crashed 17.5% today on disappointing 2026 revenue guidance of $230-260M (below market expectations). Reported $220.5M net loss in FY2025 (though $113.7M is non-cash). HETLIOZ and PONVORY revenue declining. BYSANTI and NEREUS launch execution is unproven. Director sold 30,000 shares near recent highs. Antipsychotic market is highly competitive.
Vanda Pharmaceuticals Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Results

Revenue up 9% to $216.1M but net loss ballooned to $220.5M due to $113.7M non-cash tax charge. 2026 guidance of $230-260M disappointed market.

PR Newswire

Vanda Wins FDA Approval for New Antipsychotic BYSANTI

FDA approves milsaperidone (BYSANTI) for schizophrenia and bipolar I manic episodes; commercial availability expected Q3 2026 with patents through 2044.

StockTitan

FDA Approval Pushes Vanda Pharmaceuticals Shares Higher

BYSANTI approval drives brief rally, but gains subsequently erased by guidance concerns.

StocksToTrade

Vanda Pharmaceuticals' Explosive Rally May Be More Hype Than Substance

Analysis warns that BYSANTI approval-driven rally may not be sustainable given competitive antipsychotic market and execution risks.

Invezz

Fanapt's Bipolar Inflection Meets Pipeline Optionality at Vanda Pharmaceuticals

Fanapt prescription growth of 28% signals strong commercial momentum; BYSANTI and imsidolimab provide pipeline optionality.

ENVX 42/100 Clean Energy
$4.86
-$0.22 (-4.33%)
Mkt Cap: $1.1B
Cash: $621M cash and marketable securities (Feb 2026)
Burn Rate: $95.3M annual operating cash burn FY2025; ~$27M/quarter in Q4
Timing: 6+ months — Customer qualification decision 6-12 months away (critical catalyst). Production issues suggest 12+ month delay to meaningful volume. Cash runway sufficient through 2028.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Enovix achieved 935 Wh/L energy density vs competitors' lower benchmarks in Jan 2026, validating solid-state technology. FY2025 losses narrowing ($156.74M net loss improved YoY) with $621M cash providing ~2.5 years runway. Expected customer qualification could unlock smartphone and defense contracts.
What could go wrong: Laser dicing production issues delaying volume ramp; key smartphone customer qualification still pending as of March 2026. Q1 2026 guidance only $6.5-7.5M revenue signals near-term revenue cliff. Craig Hallum cut price target due to volume production delays.
Enovix Q4 2025: Beat earnings estimates with -$0.14 EPS vs -$0.20 expected

Full-year 2025 revenue $31.82M, net loss $156.74M improved. FY2025 cash position $621M.

Enovix AI-1 battery achieves 935 Wh/L volumetric energy density in independent third-party testing

Surpasses leading commercial silicon-doped batteries, validating solid-state approach for smartphones.

Enovix / Sahm Capital

Production delays from laser dicing; customer qualification still pending

Craig Hallum maintained positive rating but cut price target to $10 due to volume production delays.

Leadership transition announced to support manufacturing scale-up

Company restructuring to address production and commercialization challenges.

York Water Company
YORW 42/100 Water Utilities
$29.87
-$0.37 (-1.22%)
Mkt Cap: $432M
Cash: Profitable regulated utility — $20.06M net earnings FY2025
Burn Rate: N/A — profitable ($20.06M net earnings, -1.31% YoY decline)
Timing: This quarter — New rate structure effective March 1, 2026 should begin flowing through in Q1 results. Shareholder meeting May 4 with director elections. This is a steady dividend growth story, not a near-term catalyst play.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Recent rate settlement approved Feb 19, effective March 1 — removes near-term regulatory uncertainty and enables recovery of infrastructure investments. 39 insider buys with zero sells in past year is the strongest insider confidence signal in batch 6. 621 consecutive dividend payments (155+ years) demonstrates unmatched stability. $48.7M annual capex program builds rate base for future growth. Regulated monopoly in York, PA service territory provides pricing power. Rate base CAGR of 5-10% supports 2-3% dividend growth.
What could go wrong: Stock down 9% from $33 to $30 in downtrend despite rate settlement approval. Net income declined 1.31% YoY despite revenue growth — heavy capex depressing near-term profitability. Limited growth as small regional water utility ($77.5M revenue). 3.37% revenue growth only modestly above inflation. Utilities underperform in rising rate environments. Small market cap limits institutional interest and liquidity.
The York Water Company Announces Settlement of Rate Request

PA PUC approved rate adjustment effective March 1, 2026 — enables recovery of infrastructure investments and removes regulatory uncertainty.

York Water IR

The York Water Company Announces 2025 Annual and Fourth Quarter Results

Revenue $77.49M (+$2.5M), but net income dipped $267K due to heavy $48.7M capital investment program.

York Water IR

York Water Declares Q1 2026 Dividend — 621st Consecutive Payment

Continued 155+ year dividend streak with $0.2280/share quarterly payment.

York Water IR

York Water CEO Joseph Hand Purchases Shares at $31.55

CEO adds to position at current levels — part of pattern of 39 insider purchases with zero sells over past year.

Denison Mines
DNN 41/100 Mining / Uranium
$3.33
-$0.19 (-5.40%)
Mkt Cap: $3.0B
Cash: $471.3M (early March 2026)
Burn Rate: $25M quarterly run-rate expenses; negative EBITDA plus $159.6M annual interest on convertible debt
Timing: This month — Phoenix construction start March 2026 is immediate catalyst. Production 2028 timeline is 24+ months away. Near-term catalysts are operational updates and uranium price momentum.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Phoenix mine FID is major catalyst with $600M capex now funded and construction starting March 2026. McClean North ramping production (648K lbs in 2025) validates ISR technology. Supply-deficit uranium market targeting $80+/lb prices creates tailwinds for 2028 first production target.
What could go wrong: Massive interest expense burden ($159.6M annually) from convertible debt erodes cash runway despite $471M balance. $600M capex for Phoenix creates execution risk; two-year timeline to mid-2028 production is long. Stock down 20%+ from January highs signals market skepticism.
Final Investment Decision for Phoenix ISR uranium mine, construction starts March 2026

DNN board approved Phoenix project; CNSC approved EIA Feb 19. $600M capex estimate. Construction ~2 years to mid-2028 first production.

PRNewswire / Denison Mines

McClean North uranium production ramps; Cosa/DNN winter 2026 drilling ongoing

McClean North produced 648,558 lbs U3O8 in 2025. Joint ventures advancing Darby and Murphy Lake North drilling.

Denison Mines

Uranium market trends upward; supply deficit accelerating into 2026

Uranium prices jumped above $80/lb Jan 2026. Market rallied 28% from lows; supply deficit converging with reactor demand and data center construction.

Five9 Inc.
FIVN 40/100 AI / Software
$15.53
-$0.27 (-1.71%)
Mkt Cap: $1.2B
Cash: $697M total cash and investments
Burn Rate: Profitable — guiding GAAP net income $75.5-83.2M for FY2026
Timing: This quarter — Q1 2026 earnings expected late April/May. Need to see AI revenue acceleration under new CEO leadership. CEO's first full quarter.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: AI revenue growing 50% YoY and surpassing $100M proves the AI pivot is working. $697M cash with $175M expected FCF provides strong financial position. New CEO from Nintex/Avalara brings AI-centric platform-building expertise. 210+ enterprise customers at >$1M ARR each creates a sticky, high-value revenue base. Non-GAAP EPS guidance midpoint of $3.18 on a $15.53 stock = ~5x P/E.
What could go wrong: Overall revenue growth of only 8-10% is slow for a $1.2B market cap tech company. Cloud contact center market is competitive (Genesys, NICE, Twilio, Amazon Connect). Stock has declined ~12% over 20 sessions. New CEO means strategy transition risk. AI cannibalization concern — if AI agents replace human agents, Five9's per-seat model could shrink.
Five9 outlines $1.254B 2026 revenue target and AI acceleration with new CEO

New CEO Amit Mathradas outlined AI-driven growth strategy with 2026 revenue guidance of $1.247-1.261B and aggressive AI investment plans.

Seeking Alpha

Five9's AI revenue jumps 50% as cash flow and 2026 outlook issued

Enterprise AI revenue surpassed $100M growing 50% YoY. Record full-year 2025 revenue of $1.15B with strong FCF generation.

StockTitan

Five9 Expands Fusion To Deepen AI Partnerships And CX Orchestration Role

Launched expanded partner program with new ISV and embedded technology partnerships aimed at AI-driven customer experiences.

Simply Wall St

Can Five9 Turn a New AI-Focused CEO Into Lasting Competitive Advantage?

Analysis of whether new CEO's AI platform-building background can transform Five9's competitive position in cloud contact centers.

Archer Aviation
ACHR 40/100 Aerospace / eVTOL
$5.76
-$0.25 (-4.16%)
Mkt Cap: $4.3B
Cash: $1.96B (cash/equivalents/short-term investments, FY2025 end; up $1.13B YoY)
Burn Rate: $618.2M net loss FY2025. Q1 2026 adj. EBITDA guidance -$160M to -$180M (worse than -$110M estimates). Annualized $640-720M
Timing: 6+ months — Full FAA type certification likely 2028. Near-term: UAE flights expected H1 2026, defense revenue and White House pilot operations starting H2 2026. Cash burn acceleration is the immediate concern.
What Changed: Q1 2026 burn guidance WORSE than expected (-$160-180M vs -$110M). Hawthorne airport acquired as LA hub (NEW). NVIDIA IGX Thor partnership announced at CES 2026 (NEW). FY2026 revenue forecast of $62M from initial operations is NEW. Cash position confirmed at $1.96B. Stock down 17% from 30-day high ($6.96 → $5.76).
Bull case: Furthest along in FAA certification process — 100% Means of Compliance acceptance is a concrete regulatory moat no competitor has matched. White House pilot program validates technology at highest level. NVIDIA partnership (IGX Thor) brings cutting-edge AI. Hawthorne airport acquisition creates LA operations hub. Analyst consensus 'Strong Buy' with $13.20 target (129% upside). $1.96B cash removes near-term financing risk.
What could go wrong: BURN IS ACCELERATING: Q1 2026 guidance of -$160M to -$180M is 45-64% worse than analyst expectations of -$110M. At this rate, $1.96B lasts only ~13 months. Full FAA type certification likely not until 2028. Pre-revenue with zero income. $4.3B market cap on no revenue requires leap of faith. Additional capital raises and dilution are virtually certain before certification.
Archer Aviation Achieves 100% FAA Means of Compliance Acceptance

First eVTOL company to achieve this milestone. Concrete regulatory moat in certification race.

Archer Aviation IR

Archer Q4 2025 Results: Net Loss $138M, Q1 2026 Guidance Worse Than Expected

Adj. EBITDA guidance of -$160M to -$180M significantly exceeded analyst estimates of -$110M. Stock fell on burn acceleration.

Archer Aviation IR

Archer Partners with NVIDIA to Build Aviation AI with IGX Thor Platform

CES 2026 announcement. Deploying at Hawthorne airport as test bed for LA air taxi AI integration.

Archer Aviation IR

Archer Selected for White House eVTOL Pilot Program in Texas, Florida, New York

Government-backed pilot operations targeting H2 2026. Validates technology and regulatory pathway.

Archer Aviation IR

Archer Acquires Hawthorne Airport as LA Air Taxi Operations Hub

Central LA location secured for commercial operations. Part of broader infrastructure buildout for air taxi network.

JinkoSolar Holding
JKS 40/100 Clean Energy
$23.40
-$0.33 (-1.39%)
Mkt Cap: $1.2B
Cash: $3.29B cash (Q3 2025); net debt $3.1B; total debt $6.4B
Burn Rate: 2025 subsidiary loss RMB 6.79B; parent company profitability varies by segment
Timing: This quarter — Jacksonville production launch May 2026, 1H shipment data release. Q3-Q4 2026: energy storage contribution ramp and high-power module margin realization.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: JinkoSolar transitioning to high-margin products (60%+ mix) and energy storage (doubling in 2026, 15-20% gross margin). Jacksonville capacity launch May 2026 reduces tariff exposure. ESS cybersecurity certification validates enterprise readiness; UI-scale deals expand addressable market.
What could go wrong: 2025 subsidiary loss of RMB 6.79B reflects severe solar module price compression and overcapacity. 2024 gross margin collapsed to 10.9% from 16.0%. Jacksonville ramp execution risk high. Net debt of $3.1B limits strategic flexibility.
Jiangxi Jinko Reports RMB 6.79B Loss in 2025, CEO Kangping Chen Resigns

Subsidiary loss vs RMB 98.9M profit in 2024; management restructuring and new CEO Haiyun Cao appointed March 16, 2026.

StockTitan / TipRanks

Jinko ESS Receives IEC 62443-2-4 Cybersecurity Certification; JKS Stock Up 10%

North American energy storage unit achieves enterprise-grade cybersecurity validation for UI-scale deployment.

Nasdaq / Exida

Jacksonville FL Solar Module Production Facility Scheduled to Launch May 1, 2026

US-based manufacturing reduces tariff exposure and supports domestic supply chain resilience.

JinkoSolar IR

Nextpower Enters Multi-Year Gigawatt-Scale Supply Agreement with Jinko Solar (U.S.) Industries

Advanced solar module supply for steel frame ecosystem; validates product differentiation vs commodity module competition.

Sidus Space
SIDU 39/100 Space / Data
$2.31
+$0.02 (+0.87%)
Mkt Cap: $154M
Cash: $12.73M (as of Jan 2, 2026)
Burn Rate: $23.75M net loss TTM; ~$6M quarterly
Timing: This quarter — Cash runway is the immediate concern. LizzieSat-4 launch later in 2026. SHIELD task order awards could be transformative but timing uncertain. Capital raise likely needed within 6 months.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: SHIELD IDIQ contract ($151B framework) could transform this micro-cap with defense task orders. LizzieSat constellation demonstrating real capabilities (sub-5-meter imagery). Multiple satellite launches planned for 2026. Insider buying signals confidence. $25M offering provides near-term bridge funding.
What could go wrong: CRITICAL: Only $12.73M cash with $6M quarterly burn = ~6 months runway. Revenue declining (-21.64% YoY) to just $3.62M TTM. $154M market cap on minimal revenue. SHIELD is framework only — no guaranteed task orders. Will need additional capital raises, creating dilution risk.
Sidus Space Demonstrates Sub-5-Meter Resolution Imagery from LizzieSat-3

HEO USA's Holmes Mk1 camera successfully imaged over Victoria, Australia. Validates on-orbit capability for defense and commercial Earth observation.

Sidus Space IR

Sidus Space Signs MOU with Simera Sense for AI-Enabled Hyperspectral Imaging

Combining Simera xScape optics with Sidus FeatherEdge hardware. Expands satellite payload capabilities.

Sidus Space IR

LizzieSat-4 Payload Integration Milestone Completed with Maris-Tech

Payload testing underway for launch later in 2026. Continues constellation build-out.

Sidus Space IR

Sidus Space Selected for $151B MDA SHIELD IDIQ Contract

Missile Defense Agency framework contract opens potential defense task orders for satellite capabilities.

Sidus Space IR

Puma Biotechnology
PBYI 38/100 Biotech
$6.12
-$0.03 (-0.49%)
Mkt Cap: $311M
Cash: $97.5M cash and securities at year-end 2025; $22.5M outstanding debt
Burn Rate: Not applicable — profitable company. FY2025 net income $31.1M. 2026 guidance: net income $10-13M.
Timing: This quarter — ALISCA-Breast1 and ALISCA-Lung1 interim data both expected Q2 2026. Q1 2026 earnings will test whether seasonal weakness matches guidance. Stock is range-bound at $6.12 with limited near-term volatility catalyst.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: PBYI is a profitable commercial-stage biotech with $228M revenue and $31M net income in FY2025 — rare for small-cap biotech. Alisertib pipeline (breast and lung) could significantly expand TAM beyond NERLYNX's HER2+ breast cancer niche. Q2 2026 interim data from two trials creates near-term catalysts. NBI inclusion brings index fund buying.
What could go wrong: 2026 guidance of $194-198M NERLYNX revenue is below FY2025's $204M, reflecting gross-to-net headwinds (27.5-28.5% vs prior). Q1 2026 expected loss of $8-10M signals seasonal weakness. R&D spending rising 30-35% pressures margins. NERLYNX faces generic competition risk as a single-product company.
Puma Biotechnology Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Results

Q4 EPS of $0.26 beat estimates; FY2025 revenue $228.4M with net income $31.1M. 2026 guidance: $194-198M NERLYNX revenue.

Puma Biotechnology IR

Puma anticipates $194M-$198M NERLYNX revenue for 2026 while advancing alisertib clinical programs

Revenue guidance below FY2025 due to gross-to-net headwinds; alisertib programs advancing with interim data expected Q2 2026.

Seeking Alpha

Puma Biotechnology Added to the Nasdaq Biotechnology Index

NBI inclusion effective December 19, 2025 brings index fund buying and increased institutional visibility.

Insider Activity: unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown
WM Technology Inc.
MAPS 38/100 Cannabis
$0.64
-$0.00 (-0.73%)
Mkt Cap: $111M
Cash: $62M (end of FY2025, +20% YoY from ~$52M)
Burn Rate: Near breakeven — $3M net income FY2025, $40M adjusted EBITDA
Timing: 6+ months — cannabis rescheduling implementation timeline is unclear. SAFE Banking Act progress needed for meaningful sector improvement. Revenue decline trend needs to reverse before stock re-rates. No imminent binary catalyst.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: WM Technology (Weedmaps) is the dominant cannabis marketplace/technology platform with $175M revenue, $40M adj. EBITDA, and $62M cash — one of the very few profitable companies in cannabis. Cannabis rescheduling to Schedule III (Dec 2025 executive order) could eventually expand addressable market as operators gain access to traditional banking and advertising. At $111M market cap on $175M revenue (0.6x sales) and $40M EBITDA (2.8x), the stock is incredibly cheap if the sector stabilizes.
What could go wrong: Revenue declining 10% YoY in Q4 with further sequential decline guided for Q1 — the cannabis market is shrinking, not growing. EPS miss of -175% shows execution challenges. Management refusing to provide 2026 EBITDA guidance signals uncertainty. Cannabis rescheduling may actually hurt Weedmaps by enabling mainstream advertising platforms (Google, Meta) to compete for cannabis ad spend. Client base under financial pressure — many MSOs are struggling or failing (see AYRWF).
WM Technology Q4 2025 Earnings: Revenue Falls, Margins Compress

Q4 revenue $43M (-10% YoY), EPS -$0.03 vs $0.04 expected. Full year $175M revenue with $40M adj. EBITDA.

Investing.com

WM Technology Inc (MAPS) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Highlights: Navigating Challenges and Seizing Opportunities

Management highlighted growing cash position (+20%) but acknowledged challenging client environment. No 2026 EBITDA guidance provided.

GuruFocus

What Cannabis Investors Can Expect in 2026 After Marijuana Rescheduling

Schedule III rescheduling signed but implementation timeline unclear. 280E tax relief may benefit larger operators more than expected.

MJ Biz Daily

WM Technology Details Cannabis Platform and Risks in 10-K Filing

Annual report details dependency on cannabis regulatory environment and risks from mainstream platform competition post-rescheduling.

SEC Filing (10-K)

QS 35/100 Battery Tech
$6.59
-$0.30 (-4.35%)
Mkt Cap: $4.0B
Cash: $970.8M cash and marketable securities; $1.0B liquid; net cash $930.74M (Feb 2026)
Burn Rate: $242.5M operating cash burn FY2025; ~$60M+ per quarter run rate
Timing: 6+ months — Vehicle testing milestone 2026 (positive). Potential licensing revenue ramp 2027-2028. Burn rate requires continued capital raises or partnership funding every 12-18 months.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: QuantumScape achieved first-ever $19.5M customer billings in FY2025 and inaugurated Eagle Line pilot production (Feb 2026), proving manufacturing repeatability. $970.8M cash + $130.7M PowerCo partnership + Volkswagen licensing support multi-year runway through 2029.
What could go wrong: Adjusted EBITDA loss $252.3M in FY2025; guidance $250-275M loss for FY2026 shows no improvement. Insiders selling heavily (-$37.45M net past 90 days). Only $5M projected revenue 2026 vs $60M+ quarterly burn — no near-term profitability path. 7 analysts = Sell consensus.
QuantumScape inaugurates Eagle Line pilot production facility with Cobra separator process

Automated production suite operational, demonstrating manufacturing repeatability at scale.

QuantumScape IR

Q4 2025 earnings: $19.5M FY2025 customer billings (first revenue), $435.1M net loss

First customer billings achieved; $970.8M cash; FY2026 guidance $250-275M adjusted EBITDA loss.

Board strengthening: Geoff Ribar (ex-NVIDIA CFO) joins Jan 2026; Ross Niebergall joins March 2026

Semiconductor and defense expertise added to board ahead of vehicle testing milestone.

QuantumScape IR

Officers selling while company burns $60M+/quarter. Negative confidence signal.

Fate Therapeutics
FATE 35/100 Biotech
$1.10
+$0.02 (+1.85%)
Mkt Cap: $128M
Cash: $205.1M (cash, equivalents, and investments at year-end 2025)
Burn Rate: ~$112M annualized free cash flow burn (FY2025); operating expenses reduced 30% YoY
Timing: 6+ months — FT819 registrational trial initiation in 2026, but meaningful clinical data likely 2027+. No near-term binary catalyst. Stock likely range-bound absent partnership or financing news.
What Changed: Price continued downward drift from $1.08 to $1.10 (+1.85% today, slight bounce). Cash runway and burn rate unchanged from prior deep dive. FT836 colorectal cancer data is new positive signal. No material change in thesis — still a long-duration bet on iPSC platform.
Bull case: FT819 has RMAT designation for SLE (lupus) and plans for registrational trial in 2026 — autoimmune CAR T is a massive market. iPSC platform enables truly off-the-shelf manufacturing at scale, differentiating from autologous competitors. 30% OpEx reduction shows discipline. FT836 early solid tumor activity is encouraging.
What could go wrong: Cash runway of ~22 months at $112M annual burn is concerning — will likely need to raise capital in 2026-2027, risking dilution at $1.10/share ($128M market cap). Stock is in persistent downtrend (from $1.51 to $1.10 over 20 days). iPSC manufacturing complexity has led to clinical delays historically. Competition from Allogene, Caribou in allogeneic space.
Fate Therapeutics Presents Updated Phase 1 Clinical Data of FT819 Off-the-shelf CAR T-cell Product Candidate for Systemic Lupus Erythematosus

FT819 demonstrates deep B-cell depletion in SLE patients using fludarabine-free conditioning; supports registrational trial plans for 2026.

Fate Therapeutics IR

Fate Therapeutics Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Results and Business Updates

Cash of $205.1M with 30% OpEx reduction; runway into year-end 2027. FT836 shows early solid tumor activity.

GlobeNewsWire

Fate Therapeutics to Participate in Leerink Partners 2026 Global Healthcare Conference

Management will present at major healthcare investor conference, potential for partnership or financing discussions.

GlobeNewsWire

Artesian Resources
ARTNA 35/100 Water Utilities
$30.50
-$1.10 (-3.48%)
Mkt Cap: $314M
Cash: Profitable regulated utility — $22.8M net income FY2025
Burn Rate: N/A — profitable ($22.8M net income, +11.9% YoY)
Timing: 6+ months — No imminent catalyst. Next rate case filing and infrastructure investment recovery drive long-term thesis. Stock decline may represent broader utility sector rotation, not fundamental deterioration.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Solid utility with 11.9% net income growth outpacing 4.6% revenue growth — operational leverage improving. 133 consecutive quarterly dividends with ~4.1% yield provides income floor. $58.8M annual infrastructure investment builds regulated rate base for future rate increases. Essential services (water/wastewater) provide defensive revenue characteristics. Delaware/Pennsylvania service territory with steady population.
What could go wrong: Stock down 10% from $34.05 to $30.50 in 20-day downtrend. All insider activity is selling (6 sells, 0 buys in past year) — no management confidence signals. Limited growth potential as small regional utility. No major rate case or catalyst announced for 2026. 4.6% revenue growth barely exceeds inflation. Utility sector underperforms in rising rate environment.
Artesian Resources Corporation Reports 2025 Year-End Earnings and Fourth Quarter Results

Revenue $112.9M (+4.6%), net income $22.8M (+11.9%), diluted EPS $2.21. Infrastructure investment of $58.8M deployed.

GlobeNewsWire

Artesian Resources Declares First Quarter 2026 Common Stock Dividend

133rd consecutive quarterly dividend of $0.3136/share — demonstrates stability and commitment to shareholder returns.

GlobeNewsWire

Artesian Resources Director Sells $62K in Stock

Director Dian Taylor disposed of 2,000 shares at $31.00 on March 19 — part of ongoing insider selling pattern with zero purchases.

PLUG 34/100 Clean Energy
$2.23
-$0.17 (-7.08%)
Mkt Cap: $3.1B
Cash: $166M unrestricted cash (Q3 2025), additional $132.5M from Stream Data Centers asset sale; ~$300M total liquidity
Burn Rate: $90M net cash used in Q3 2025 (49% YoY improvement); targeting $275M liquidity improvement initiative
Timing: 6+ months — Q4 2026 EBITDAS positive target is 9+ months away. Next earnings May 13, 2026. Lawsuit deadline April 3, 2026 creates near-term uncertainty. Leadership transition adds execution risk.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: 2025 revenue growth 17.6% Q4 YoY with margin expansion. $275M total liquidity improvement initiative underway (asset sales, restricted cash, cost cuts). Targeting EBITDAS positive Q4 2026 and operating income by end 2027.
What could go wrong: Securities class action filed alleging misrepresentation of $1.66B DOE loan approval. Heavy cash burn continues ($90M Q3 2025 despite improvements). Share authorization crisis — less than 0.4% authorized shares remaining as of March 2026; dilution likely imminent.
Plug Power Reports Q4 and Full Year 2025 Results with Strong Sales Growth and Margin Expansion

2025 full-year revenue $710M with Q4 margin positive. Stock popped on March 2, 2026 following earnings release.

Plug Power IR

Plug Power Executes $132.5 Million Definitive Agreement with Stream Data Centers

Asset monetization deal providing $132.5M-$142M in proceeds as part of $275M liquidity improvement initiative.

Plug Power IR

PLUG DEADLINE ALERT: Faruqi & Faruqi Reminds Plug Power Investors of Securities Class Action Deadline

Class action lawsuit over DOE loan misrepresentation ($1.66B facility). Claims executives misled investors about loan approval likelihood.

PR Newswire

Plug Power Accelerates Reconvened Session of Special Meeting of Stockholders

Shareholder vote on charter amendments and share authorization increase. Company has less than 0.4% authorized shares remaining.

BigBear.ai
BBAI 32/100 AI / Defense
$3.58
-$0.15 (-4.02%)
Mkt Cap: $1.7B
Cash: $462M cash and investments (after settling $182M in 2029 Notes)
Burn Rate: Narrowing losses — Q4 2025 net loss $5.8M (vs $138.2M in Q4 2024)
Timing: 6+ months — No near-term catalysts beyond normal contract flow. Defense AI spending increases are real but competition (Palantir, Pentagon's own tools) is intensifying. Next earnings expected May/June 2026.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: $462M cash with 90% debt reduction puts BBAI in its strongest-ever financial position. $385M backlog provides visibility. DoD CDAO contract and UAE expansion show defense/international AI demand. At $1.7B market cap, if 2026 revenue hits $165M high-end, trades at ~10x sales — reasonable for defense AI. Losses narrowing dramatically ($5.8M vs $138.2M Q4 YoY).
What could go wrong: Revenue guidance of only 17% growth is weak for an AI company — market expects more. 18 insider sales with zero buys over 12 months is a persistent red flag. Pentagon's GenAI.mil platform launched as a free alternative, potentially commoditizing BBAI's offerings. Stock down ~14% over 20 sessions and -30% over 3 months. Revenue actually declined in 2025, and 2026 growth is a recovery, not acceleration.
BigBear.ai Announces Fourth Quarter 2025 Results; Releases 2026 Financial Outlook

Q4 loss narrowed to $5.8M from $138.2M YoY. 2026 guidance of $135-165M revenue. $462M cash after 90% debt reduction.

BusinessWire

BigBear.ai Wins Department of Defense Contract to Prototype Near-Peer Adversary Geopolitical Risk Analysis for CDAO

Won DoD contract to advance VANE AI prototype for geopolitical risk analysis, demonstrating continued government AI demand.

BigBear.ai IR

Did The Pentagon-OpenAI Deal Kill BigBear?

Hunterbrook analysis examines whether Pentagon's GenAI.mil platform, launched 29 days after BBAI acquisition, undercuts BigBear.ai's competitive position.

Hunterbrook

BigBear.ai Establishes UAE Operations as It Seeks Growth Beyond U.S. Defense Contracts

Opened Abu Dhabi office and formed partnerships with UAE entities including Vigilix, Easy Lease, and AD Ports Group to diversify revenue.

Planet 13 Holdings
PLNH 32/100 Cannabis
$0.15
-$0.01 (-7.59%)
Mkt Cap: $48M
Cash: $15.9M (end of Q2 2025, down from $23.4M at end of 2024). Q3/Q4 data not yet available.
Burn Rate: $44M net loss Q3 2025 (includes $29.8M non-cash impairment). Adjusted burn ~$14M/quarter.
Timing: This week — Q4 2025 earnings due March 31, 2026 (8 days). This is a critical report: will show whether California exit improves financials or if the decline continues. Cash position update will be closely watched.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: California exit simplifies operations and cuts costs — Planet 13 can now focus on its core Nevada, Florida, and Illinois markets. The company's Las Vegas superstore is an iconic cannabis retail brand with tourist traffic. FY2024 showed 18% revenue growth before the Q2-Q3 2025 decline. At $48M market cap on $116M annual revenue, the stock trades at just 0.4x sales. Earnings March 31 could show improvement from California cost elimination.
What could go wrong: CRITICAL: Cash position of $15.9M with ~$14M quarterly burn means runway may be under 6 months. Revenue declining sharply (-27.6% in Q3). Gross margin collapsed from 51.9% to 21.3%. Cannabis sector remains in a death spiral with no federal legalization progress. $29.8M impairment signals management acknowledges value destruction. Stock at $0.15 is near terminal levels for OTC-listed cannabis companies.
Planet 13 Announces Q3 2025 Financial Results

Revenue declined 27.6% to $23.3M. Net loss of $44M includes $29.8M impairment. Gross margin collapsed to 21.3%.

StockTitan

Planet 13 Announces Q2 2025 Financial Results

Revenue declined 13.6% to $26.9M with Adjusted EBITDA loss of $2.4M. Cannabis retail headwinds persist.

StockTitan

Planet 13 Completes California Exit with Coalinga Property Sale

Closed sale of Coalinga cultivation property, substantially completing California exit. Simplifies operations to NV/FL/IL.

Insider Activity: unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown unknown
C3.ai Inc.
AI 28/100 AI / Software
$8.47
-$0.20 (-2.31%)
Mkt Cap: $1.2B
Cash: $621.9M (down from $675M in Q2 FY2026)
Burn Rate: High — negative $56.2M free cash flow in Q3; ~$80M burned since fiscal year start
Timing: 6+ months — Restructuring benefits won't materialize until H2 FY2027. Need multiple quarters to prove revenue stabilization. Stock is in freefall mode with no near-term catalyst.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: $621.9M cash provides ~2 years of runway even at current burn rates. Restructuring (26% workforce cut + 30% non-employee cost reduction) targeting $135M in savings should dramatically improve unit economics by H2 FY2027. If new CEO Ehikian can execute the turnaround, the stock is priced for failure at $1.2B market cap on an AI platform with government/defense customers.
What could go wrong: Revenue declining from $75.1M to $53.3M quarter-over-quarter is alarming — this isn't just a miss, it's deterioration. 26% layoffs signal deeper problems than cost optimization. Heavy insider selling ($7.5M) including founder/chairman selling $4.4M signals low confidence. Competition from hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP) offering enterprise AI makes C3.ai's value proposition questionable. Cash burn of $56M/quarter means runway is finite.
C3 AI shares plummet as company announces global layoffs

Stock sank after massive earnings miss ($53.3M vs $75.9M expected) and announcement of 26% workforce reduction under new CEO's restructuring.

CNBC

C3.ai slashes 26% of staff as CEO admits failure to deliver and 'burning too much money'

New CEO Ehikian acknowledged cost structure was unsustainably high and announced restructuring targeting $135M in operating expense savings.

Yahoo Finance

C3.ai Layoffs Will Impact Quarter of Company

Bloomberg reports the layoffs affect approximately 300 of C3.ai's 1,200 employees following CEO transition from Thomas Siebel.

Bloomberg

Assessing C3.ai Valuation After Sharp Revenue Drop And Restructuring Plan

Analysis argues the revenue decline is structural, not cyclical, and questions whether restructuring can salvage the business model.

Simply Wall St

Vertical Aerospace
EVTL 26/100 Aerospace / eVTOL
$3.59
-$0.12 (-3.23%)
Mkt Cap: $358M
Cash: £69M (~$93M) as of Dec 31, 2025 (down from $139M as of Aug 2025)
Burn Rate: Estimated $700M additional capital needed for VX4/Valo certification by 2028
Timing: 6+ months — Certification not expected until 2028. Cash crunch approaching mid-2026. Earnings release Mar 24 may provide updated funding plans. No near-term operational catalyst.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: 1,500 firm order backlog from major airlines (American Airlines, Japan Airlines, AirAsia, GOL). UK CAA 'Permit to Fly' with self-approval privileges shows regulatory confidence. Marubeni partnership for Japan/Osaka Bay operations. Battery pilot production line launched. Diverse international customer base reduces single-market risk.
What could go wrong: CRITICAL CASH SITUATION: ~$93M cash funded only through mid-2026 with $700M more needed for certification. Certification pushed to 2028 — 2+ years away with no near-term revenue. No FAA type certification initiated. $358M market cap on zero revenue and existential funding risk. Dilution or potential restructuring likely necessary.
Vertical Aerospace Launches Battery Pilot Production Line for Valo Aircraft

Manufacturing milestone for electric propulsion system. Part of production readiness ahead of certification.

Vertical Aerospace IR

Vertical Aerospace to Report FY 2025 Business Strategy Update on March 24

Earnings and strategy update expected. Market watching for updated funding plans and certification timeline.

BusinessWire

JetSetGo Signs MOU for 50 Valo Aircraft Pre-Order for India Operations

New customer commitment adds to 1,500 unit backlog. Expands geographic reach into Indian market.

Vertical Aerospace IR

Evolito Named Long-Term Electric Propulsion Partner for Valo

Joint UK CAA and EASA validation pathway targeting 2028 commercial service. Secures critical propulsion supply chain.

Vertical Aerospace IR

Lithium Americas
LAC 26/100 Mining / Lithium
$3.71
-$0.30 (-7.48%)
Mkt Cap: $1.1B
Cash: $905.6M (total cash + restricted cash, including $412.6M at Thacker Pass JV level)
Burn Rate: $86M net loss FY2025 (+102% YoY), plus $1.3-1.6B CapEx planned for 2026
Timing: 6+ months — Q4 2026 early commissioning is next milestone. First production late 2027. Very long timeline with continuous dilution risk along the way.
What Changed: Price dropped from $4.01 to $3.71 (-7.48% today, -19% over 20 days). Persistent downtrend continues. No new catalysts since last deep dive. Construction timeline unchanged. Dilution pressure ongoing.
Bull case: Thacker Pass is the largest known lithium deposit in the US — strategically critical for EV supply chain independence. $905.6M cash with DOE loan backing funds construction. Engineering 93% complete with commissioning starting Q4 2026. GM partnership provides demand certainty. IRA policy tailwinds for domestic critical minerals. If lithium prices recover, the deposit's economics improve dramatically.
What could go wrong: MASSIVE dilution story: 100.7M shares issued ($591M ATM) plus DOE warrants for 5% equity at $0.01. $1.3-1.6B CapEx in 2026 exceeds current $906M cash — more financing (and dilution) is coming. First production not until late 2027 at earliest. Lithium prices remain near cycle lows. Mining construction projects routinely face cost overruns. Stock down 19% in 20 days with big_move flag.
Lithium Americas Sets $1.3-$1.6 Billion 2026 Capex as Thacker Pass Construction Advances

Peak construction year with 1,800 workers. Engineering 93% complete. But CapEx exceeds cash on hand.

TipRanks

Lithium Americas Highlights Strong Liquidity and Thacker Pass Progress

$905.6M cash position with DOE loan backing. But 2026 CapEx of $1.3-1.6B means more financing likely needed.

TipRanks

Lithium Americas Details Thacker Pass Timeline, Capex, and Execution Risks

Mechanical completion late 2027, first production late 2027. Budget and timeline risk flagged by analysts.

Simply Wall St

Why Lithium Americas Could Be a 2030 Power Play — Not a 2025 One

Long-term strategic value of US lithium independence is clear, but near-term returns unlikely given construction timeline and dilution.

Argo Blockchain
ARBK 18/100 Crypto Mining
$2.91
+$0.01 (+0.42%)
Mkt Cap: $969,952
Cash: ~$753K available cash (as of Sep 2025). CRITICALLY LOW. After restructuring, $40M of 8.75% senior notes equitized, leaving only $472K mortgage on Baie-Comeau facility.
Burn Rate: $25.7M net loss TTM. Operations supported by Growler facility financing.
Timing: 6+ months — next earnings May 1, 2026. No near-term catalysts. Bitcoin price is the only variable that matters, and even then, 87.5% of any upside accrues to Growler Mining, not public shareholders.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: Post-restructuring, Argo's balance sheet is clean with only a $472K mortgage remaining after equitizing $40M in debt. Growler Mining's 87.5% ownership provides a well-capitalized backstop. Revenue of $24M from Bitcoin mining operations is real. If Bitcoin sustains above $80K+, mining economics improve significantly. The Baie-Comeau facility uses cheap Quebec hydroelectric power, giving a cost advantage.
What could go wrong: CRITICAL: Only $753K in available cash — this company survives month-to-month on Growler's support. 25.25 billion new shares issued means original equity holders are diluted to near-zero. Market cap of ~$970K is essentially a shell. Revenue declining to ~$20.7M forecast. EPS of -$81.73 (loss per ADS) shows catastrophic per-share economics post-dilution. Trading volume of only 6,087 shares/day means zero liquidity.
Argo Blockchain Completes Court-Sanctioned Restructuring

Growler Mining received 87.5% of shares through restructuring. 25.25B new shares issued. $40M in debt equitized.

SEC Filing

Argo Blockchain Reports Available Cash of $753K as Operations Continue Under Growler Support

Critically low cash position. Operations sustained by Growler Mining facility. Revenue declining.

SEC Filing (6-K)

Argo Blockchain ADR Trading at Sub-$1M Market Cap After Massive Dilution

25.25 billion new shares crushed per-share economics. Original equity holders effectively wiped out.

Steakholder Foods
STKH 15/100 Alt. Agriculture
$1.21
-$0.04 (-3.20%)
Mkt Cap: $6M
Cash: $1.43M cash vs. $874K debt — net cash ~$551K (CRITICAL: ~2 months runway at current burn)
Burn Rate: $8.03M annual operating loss; $6.90M operating cash burn; $715K capex
Timing: This month — Earnings April 2 will show Q4 2025 results and cash burn update. Likely going concern warning. Emergency funding or M&A required within weeks. Stock could go to zero without intervention.
What Changed: First deep dive — no prior data to compare.
Bull case: At $8.68M enterprise value, any M&A interest from a larger food/biotech company could create outsized return. Cultivated meat is a long-term secular trend with potential for 10x+ if technology matures and FDA approves. If new investor injects $20M+ at favorable terms, extends runway to enable pilot-to-commercial transition. Pure option value play at current levels.
What could go wrong: CRITICAL: Only $551K net cash with ~2 months runway — going concern risk is imminent. $10K TTM revenue means zero commercial traction. Twine Solutions subsidiary collapsed into insolvency showing poor capital allocation. Directors resigned over governance disputes. 5.69M ADS filing means massive dilution incoming for current shareholders. No FDA approval or clear regulatory pathway for cultivated meat (2-3+ year timeline minimum). Competitors (Eat Just, Upside Foods) have significant leads. Near-bankruptcy scenario most likely outcome.
Twine Solutions Subsidiary Enters Insolvency After Steakholder Halts Funding

Steakholder stopped funding Twine Solutions to refocus on core cultivated meat business, but subsidiary collapsed into insolvency.

StockTitan

Steakholder Foods Directors Resign Over Twine Solutions Administration Disputes

Board members quit over disagreements on handling of failed subsidiary — governance breakdown.

StockTitan

Steakholder Foods Files to Sell 5.69M ADSs

Dilutive equity raise filing signals desperate need for capital at depressed price — massive shareholder dilution expected.

The Markets Daily

Ayr Wellness
AYRWF 12/100 Cannabis
$0.01
-$0.00 (-12.50%)
Mkt Cap: $2M
Cash: ~$50M bridge facility at 14% interest; company in court-supervised wind-down
Burn Rate: N/A — company winding down operations and selling assets to satisfy creditors
Timing: 6+ months — asset sale process is court-supervised and will take months to years. Equity recovery is essentially zero probability. This is not a moonshot — it's a lottery ticket with terrible odds.
What Changed: Price dropped from $0.016 to $0.014 (-12.5% today, -42% over 20 days). No new restructuring developments. Court-supervised wind-down continues. Equity is essentially worthless.
Bull case: At $0.014 per share (~$2M market cap), any recovery in asset sale values beyond what's needed to satisfy $358M secured debt could theoretically provide equity upside. Florida, New Jersey, and Ohio cannabis licenses have standalone strategic value. If federal rescheduling (Schedule III executive order Dec 2025) accelerates license values before wind-down completes, there's an outside chance equity holders see something.
What could go wrong: This is a company in court-supervised liquidation with $904M in total liabilities. The $50M bridge loan at 14% interest rate demonstrates extreme financial distress. Equity holders are absolutely last in line — secured creditors, unsecured creditors, and preferred equity all get paid first. At $904M liabilities, the probability that asset sales generate enough to reach common equity is near zero. Stock may go to $0.00.
Ayr Wellness Announces Court-Supervised Restructuring Process

Company enters court-supervised wind-down to satisfy $904M in liabilities. Asset sales across 8 states to repay secured lenders.

Company Press Release

Ayr Wellness Secures $50M Bridge Facility at 14% Interest

Bridge loan from senior noteholders at distressed rate signals severe liquidity crisis. Funds operations during asset sales.

Cannabis Business Times

Cannabis MSO Ayr Wellness Stock Plummets 88% as Restructuring Looms

Stock has lost nearly 90% of its value over the past year as debt load proves unsustainable despite $464M in revenue.

MJ Biz Daily
